::: {.VPHome v-1428d186="" v-686f80a6=""}
::: {.VPHero .has-image .VPHomeHero v-686f80a6="" v-303bb580=""}
::: {.container v-303bb580=""}
::: {.main v-303bb580=""}
[OpenGradient]{.clip v-303bb580=""} {#opengradient .name v-303bb580=""}
===================================

Documentation

Vertically-integrated decentralized infrastructure for secure and
verifiable AI execution, agent and application deployment, and AI model
hosting.

::: {.actions v-303bb580=""}
::: {.action v-303bb580=""}
[Build on
OpenGradient](https://docs.opengradient.ai/developers.html){.VPButton
.medium .brand}
:::

::: {.action v-303bb580=""}
[Learn about
OpenGradient](https://docs.opengradient.ai/about.html){.VPButton .medium
.alt}
:::
:::
:::

::: {.image v-303bb580=""}
::: {.image-container v-303bb580=""}
::: {.image-bg v-303bb580=""}
:::

![OpenGradient](logo.png){.VPImage .image-src}
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.VPFeatures .VPHomeFeatures v-686f80a6="" v-a6181336=""}
::: {.container v-a6181336=""}
::: {.items v-a6181336=""}
::: {.grid-3 .item v-a6181336=""}
[](https://docs.opengradient.ai/developers/sdk.html){.VPLink .link
.no-icon .VPFeature}

::: {.icon v-a3976bdc=""}
:::

Python SDK {#python-sdk .title v-a3976bdc=""}
----------

Build applications with OpenGradient\'s verified AI infrastructure. Run
ML and LLM inference, manage models, and deploy automated workflows with
full integrity guarantees.
:::

::: {.grid-3 .item v-a6181336=""}
[](https://docs.opengradient.ai/models/model_hub.html){.VPLink .link
.no-icon .VPFeature}

::: {.icon v-a3976bdc=""}
:::

Decentralized Model Hub {#decentralized-model-hub .title v-a3976bdc=""}
-----------------------

A permissionless repository supporting all model architectures. Upload,
browse, and use models instantly available for inference on the
OpenGradient network.
:::

::: {.grid-3 .item v-a6181336=""}
[](https://docs.opengradient.ai/developers/memsync.html){.VPLink .link
.no-icon .VPFeature}

::: {.icon v-a3976bdc=""}
:::

MemSync {#memsync .title v-a3976bdc=""}
-------

Long-term memory layer for AI with persistent context management.
Automatically extract, organize, and search memories to build
personalized AI applications.
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.vp-doc .container style="" v-686f80a6="" v-8e2d4988=""}

::: {.content v-39a288b8=""}

::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._models_model_hub_ style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

Model Hub [​](index.html#model-hub){.header-anchor} {#model-hub tabindex="-1"}
===================================================

::: {.tip .custom-block .github-alert}
TIP

Explore the OpenGradient Model Hub at
[hub.opengradient.ai](https://hub.opengradient.ai)
:::

The OpenGradient Model Hub is a decentralized repository for AI models
--- a place to discover, share, version, and run models of every kind.
From lightweight regression models to full-scale LLMs, diffusion models,
and everything in between, the Hub gives every model a permanent home
backed by [Walrus](https://www.walrus.xyz/) decentralized storage.

Unlike centralized model registries, the Model Hub is
**permissionless**: anyone can upload a model and make it available for
inference on the OpenGradient network in seconds, with no gatekeepers
and no approval queues.

Why the Model Hub? [​](index.html#why-the-model-hub){.header-anchor} {#why-the-model-hub tabindex="-1"}
--------------------------------------------------------------------

Traditional model hosting is fragmented. Models are scattered across
cloud buckets, GitHub repos, and proprietary platforms --- each with its
own access patterns, versioning schemes, and limitations. The Model Hub
provides a single, decentralized alternative that combines:

-   **Permanent, decentralized storage** --- Models are stored on
    [Walrus](https://docs.opengradient.ai/learn/architecture/storage.html),
    so they can\'t be taken down, censored, or lost when a cloud
    provider changes its terms.
-   **Built-in versioning** --- Every model is organized into
    repositories with structured releases, so you can iterate without
    breaking consumers.
-   **Inference-ready by default** --- Models in ONNX format are
    immediately available for on-chain inference across the OpenGradient
    network, including Vanilla, ZKML, and LLM execution modes.
-   **Community and collaboration** --- Each model has its own
    discussion forum, tags, and social features to help the community
    learn and iterate together.
-   **Web3-native access** --- Sign in with your wallet or a traditional
    email account. Models are identified by content-addressed Blob IDs,
    making them composable with smart contracts and on-chain workflows.

What You Can Find [​](index.html#what-you-can-find){.header-anchor} {#what-you-can-find tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------------------

The Hub hosts models across a broad range of tasks and architectures:

  Category              Examples
  --------------------- ------------------------------------------------------------
  **LLMs**              Chat models, completion models, instruction-tuned variants
  **DeFi & Finance**    Volatility forecasters, dynamic fee models, risk scoring
  **Computer Vision**   Image classifiers, object detection, stable diffusion
  **Time Series**       Price prediction, anomaly detection, forecasting
  **General ML**        Regression, classification, clustering, embeddings

There are no restrictions on what you can upload --- the Hub supports
any model format for storage. For on-chain inference, models need to be
in [ONNX format](model_restrictions.html).

Getting Started [​](index.html#getting-started){.header-anchor} {#getting-started tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------------

There are two ways to interact with the Model Hub:

### Web Frontend [​](index.html#web-frontend){.header-anchor} {#web-frontend tabindex="-1"}

The [Hub website](https://hub.opengradient.ai) is the fastest way to
browse, search, and test models. It provides:

-   Full-text search with filters for tasks, tags, authors, and
    organizations
-   A built-in **Playground** for running inference directly in your
    browser
-   Model pages with descriptions, version history, files, and community
    discussions
-   One-click uploads for new models and versions

The web UI is ideal for exploring what\'s available, trying out models,
and managing your repositories visually.

### Python SDK & CLI [​](index.html#python-sdk-cli){.header-anchor} {#python-sdk-cli tabindex="-1"}

The [Python SDK](../../developers/sdk/index.html) and bundled CLI are
designed for programmatic workflows --- CI/CD pipelines, batch uploads,
scripted version management, and integration into training pipelines.
Install with:

::: {.language-bash .vp-adaptive-theme}
[bash]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
pip install opengradient
```
:::

Then create, version, and upload models from your terminal or scripts:

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import opengradient as og

hub = og.ModelHub(email="<email>", password="<password>")

# Create a new model repository
hub.create_model("my-model", model_desc="A volatility forecasting model")

# Upload a model file
hub.upload(model_path="model.onnx", model_name="my-model", version="1.00")
```
:::

See the [SDK Model Management
guide](https://docs.opengradient.ai/developers/sdk/model_management.html)
for full CLI and library documentation.

How Models Are Organized [​](index.html#how-models-are-organized){.header-anchor} {#how-models-are-organized tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Hub uses a three-level hierarchy to keep models structured and
iterable:

::: {.language- .vp-adaptive-theme}
[]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
Model Repository
├── Release v1.00
│   ├── model.onnx
│   ├── README.md
│   └── config.json
├── Release v1.01
│   ├── model.onnx  (updated)
│   └── README.md
└── Release v2.00
    ├── model_part1.onnx
    ├── model_part2.onnx
    └── README.md
```
:::

-   **Model Repository** --- The top-level container. Holds metadata
    like the model\'s name, description, task category, license, and
    tags.
-   **Model Release** --- A specific version of the model (e.g., `1.00`,
    `1.01`, `2.00`). Each release is independently usable and can
    contain release notes describing what changed.
-   **Model Files** --- The actual artifacts within a release. In simple
    cases this is a single ONNX file; for larger architectures like
    LLMs, a release can contain multiple files along with documentation
    and configs.

Learn more in [Model Management](model_management.html).

Running Inference [​](index.html#running-inference){.header-anchor} {#running-inference tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------------------

Every ONNX model on the Hub is ready for inference on the OpenGradient
network. There are multiple execution modes:

-   **Vanilla Inference** --- Standard model execution, fastest option
-   **ZKML Inference** --- Zero-knowledge proof-based execution for
    privacy-preserving, verifiable results
-   **LLM Inference** --- Chat and completion endpoints for large
    language models

You can test any model using the
[Playground](core_features.html#playground) on the Hub, or run inference
programmatically through the [Python
SDK](https://docs.opengradient.ai/developers/sdk/ml_inference.html).

::: {.note .custom-block .github-alert}
NOTE

To learn more about the decentralized storage layer that powers the Hub,
see
[Storage](https://docs.opengradient.ai/learn/architecture/storage.html).
:::

</div>
:::
:::

::: {.vp-doc ._models_model_hub_model_management style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

Model Management [​](model_management.html#model-management){.header-anchor} {#model-management tabindex="-1"}
============================================================================

Models on OpenGradient are managed through **model repositories** ---
structured containers that hold every version of a model along with its
metadata, files, and documentation.

Repository Structure [​](model_management.html#repository-structure){.header-anchor} {#repository-structure tabindex="-1"}
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A model repository represents a single model project. It contains:

-   **Name** --- A unique identifier (e.g.,
    `opengradient-1-hour-volatility-ethusdt1`)
-   **Description** --- A Markdown-formatted explanation of the model\'s
    purpose, architecture, and usage
-   **Task** --- The problem domain (e.g., DeFi Forecasting, Risk Model,
    LLM, Image Generation)
-   **License** --- The license governing usage and redistribution
-   **Tags** --- Freeform labels for discovery (e.g., `ethereum`,
    `time-series`, `onnx`)
-   **Visibility** --- Public (visible to everyone) or Private (visible
    only to you)

Within each repository, you create **releases** (versions) that hold the
actual model files.

Releases & Versioning [​](model_management.html#releases-versioning){.header-anchor} {#releases-versioning tabindex="-1"}
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each release is an independently usable snapshot of the model. Releases
follow a `major.minor` versioning scheme:

-   **v1.00** --- Initial release
-   **v1.01** --- Minor update (e.g., retrained with more data,
    hyperparameter tweak)
-   **v2.00** --- Major update (e.g., new architecture, breaking
    input/output changes)

Every release can include:

-   **Model files** --- The core model artifacts (e.g., `.onnx` files).
    For large models like LLMs, a single release can hold multiple model
    files.
-   **Documentation** --- READMEs, architecture notes, training logs
-   **Supporting files** --- Config files, example scripts, sample data,
    or anything else useful to consumers

When you create a new model repository, version `1.00` is automatically
initialized for you.

::: {.tip .custom-block .github-alert}
TIP

Consumers reference a model by its **Blob ID** --- a content-addressed
identifier for a specific file in a specific release. Uploading a new
version never changes existing Blob IDs, so existing consumers are
unaffected.
:::

Managing Models via the Web UI [​](model_management.html#managing-models-via-the-web-ui){.header-anchor} {#managing-models-via-the-web-ui tabindex="-1"}
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The [Hub website](https://hub.opengradient.ai) provides a visual
interface for the full model lifecycle:

1.  **Create** a new model repository --- set name, description, task,
    license, tags, and visibility
2.  **Upload** files to a release --- drag-and-drop or file picker, with
    duplicate detection
3.  **Create new releases** --- bump the version and add release notes
4.  **Edit metadata** --- update descriptions, tags, license, or
    visibility at any time
5.  **Rename or delete** --- available in the model settings panel

Managing Models via the SDK & CLI [​](model_management.html#managing-models-via-the-sdk-cli){.header-anchor} {#managing-models-via-the-sdk-cli tabindex="-1"}
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The [Python
SDK](https://docs.opengradient.ai/developers/sdk/model_management.html)
and CLI provide the same capabilities for scripted and automated
workflows.

### CLI [​](model_management.html#cli){.header-anchor} {#cli tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-bash .vp-adaptive-theme}
[bash]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
# Create a new model repository
opengradient create-model-repo --repo "my_model" --description "My volatility model"

# Create a new version
opengradient create-version --repo "my_model" --notes "Retrained on Q4 data"

# Upload a file to a specific version
opengradient upload-file path/to/model.onnx --repo "my_model" --version "1.01"

# List files in a version
opengradient list-files --repo "my_model" --version "1.01"
```
:::

### Python Library [​](model_management.html#python-library){.header-anchor} {#python-library tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import opengradient as og

hub = og.ModelHub(email="<email>", password="<password>")

# Create a model repository (automatically initializes v1.00)
hub.create_model("my_model", model_desc="My volatility model")

# Create a new minor version
version = hub.create_version("my_model", notes="Retrained on Q4 data")

# Upload model file
hub.upload(
    model_path="model.onnx",
    model_name="my_model",
    version=version
)

# List files
files = hub.list_files("my_model", version)
```
:::

::: {.tip .custom-block .github-alert}
TIP

Refer to the [Getting Started guide](../../developers/sdk/index.html) to
obtain your private key and credentials, and the [API
Reference](../../api_reference/python_sdk/index.html) for detailed
method signatures.
:::

</div>
:::

::: {.content v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.content-container v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._models_model_hub_model_restrictions style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

Model Restrictions [​](model_restrictions.html#model-restrictions){.header-anchor} {#model-restrictions tabindex="-1"}
==================================================================================

Supported Inference Format [​](model_restrictions.html#supported-inference-format){.header-anchor} {#supported-inference-format tabindex="-1"}
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Our file storage network can support models of any type and format.
Currently, the OpenGradient network only supports inference for models
in ONNX format. You can convert your custom models into ONNX format by
using the [ONNX converter](https://onnx.ai/onnx/intro/converters.html).
The converter supports formats described
[here](https://github.com/onnx/tutorials?tab=readme-ov-file#converting-to-onnx-format).

::: {.note .custom-block .github-alert}
NOTE

You can store and upload models in any format; however if you want to
use it for on-chain inference, it must be ONNX.
:::

Supported Model Types for ZK Inference [​](model_restrictions.html#supported-model-types-for-zk-inference){.header-anchor} {#supported-model-types-for-zk-inference tabindex="-1"}
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Using the [EZKL library](https://docs.ezkl.xyz/) there are currently
some limitations on what models can be supported for ZK Inference. The
main constrictions are:

-   The supported ONNX operator set is 9 - 18 inclusive for the latest
    EZKL version. This may require you to downgrade some operators in
    order run a ZKML inference.
-   Inputs must be of numerical typing (float, int, etc.). Any string
    type input must be encoded into a numerical type in order to be
    processed correctly.
-   Models cannot have a mix of static and dynamic (e.g. batched) shaped
    inputs. Multiple static shaped inputs, and singular batched inputs
    are accepted. The reccomendation for this scenario is to hard-code
    the constant values into your model beforehand.

</div>
:::
:::

::: {.content v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.content-container v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._models_model_hub_core_features style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

Core Features [​](core_features.html#core-features){.header-anchor} {#core-features tabindex="-1"}
===================================================================

Browsing & Discovery [​](core_features.html#browsing-discovery){.header-anchor} {#browsing-discovery tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Hub\'s [model explorer](https://hub.opengradient.ai/models) is
designed for fast, flexible discovery across the entire model catalog.

**Search** --- Find models by name, author, or content identifier using
the search bar. Advanced search qualifiers let you narrow results
further:

-   `name:volatility` --- search within model names
-   `author:OpenGradient` --- filter by publisher
-   `cid:Qm...` --- look up a model by its content identifier

**Filter by task** --- Narrow results to a specific problem domain:
LLMs, risk models, DeFi forecasting, image generation, protocol
optimization, multimodal models, and more.

**Filter by tags** --- Community and author-applied tags make it easy to
find models for specific use cases, frameworks, or datasets.

**Filter by organization** --- Browse models published by a specific
team or organization.

**Sort** --- Order results by relevance, recency, popularity (likes), or
download count to surface the models that matter most.

Model Pages [​](core_features.html#model-pages){.header-anchor} {#model-pages tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------------

Every model has a dedicated page that serves as its home on the Hub. A
model page includes:

-   **About** --- A rich description with full Markdown support
    (including LaTeX math and GFM tables). Authors can document their
    model\'s purpose, architecture, training details, and usage
    instructions.
-   **Files & Versions** --- Browse every release and its files.
    Download individual files, view file metadata, and copy Blob IDs for
    use in inference calls.
-   **Playground** --- Run the model interactively (see below).
-   **Metadata** --- Task category, license, tags, download stats, and
    like count.

Model authors also get access to a settings panel where they can update
descriptions, manage tags, control visibility (public or private),
rename the model, or delete it.

Playground [​](core_features.html#playground){.header-anchor} {#playground tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------------

The Playground is an interactive inference sandbox built into every
model page. It lets you run a model directly in your browser --- no SDK
setup, no code required.

For **ONNX models**, the Playground automatically reads the model\'s
input and output schema and generates a form with the correct field
names and types pre-populated. Model authors can customize the default
input values and add descriptions to make it easier for visitors to test
the model with realistic data.

For **LLM models**, the Playground provides a chat interface where you
can send messages and see responses in real time.

The Playground calls inference on the OpenGradient network, so results
are identical to what you\'d get through the SDK or a smart contract ---
including the blockchain transaction hash that records the execution.

::: {.tip .custom-block .github-alert}
TIP

Try the Playground on any model --- for example: [OpenGradient
Volatility Model
Playground](https://hub.opengradient.ai/models/OpenGradient/opengradient-1-hour-volatility-ethusdt1/playground)
:::

Versioning [​](core_features.html#versioning){.header-anchor} {#versioning tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------------

Models evolve. The Hub\'s versioning system is designed to make
iteration seamless without breaking existing consumers.

Each model repository supports **semantic versioning** with major and
minor releases:

-   **Minor versions** (e.g., `1.00` → `1.01`) --- incremental
    improvements, retraining runs, or small fixes
-   **Major versions** (e.g., `1.01` → `2.00`) --- architectural
    changes, breaking input/output changes, or significant upgrades

Every version includes:

-   Its own set of files (model artifacts, documentation, configs)
-   Optional release notes describing what changed
-   Independent Blob IDs, so consumers referencing a specific version
    are never affected by newer uploads

You can create and manage versions through the [web
UI](https://hub.opengradient.ai) or the [Python
SDK](https://docs.opengradient.ai/developers/sdk/model_management.html).

Organizations [​](core_features.html#organizations){.header-anchor} {#organizations tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------------------

Organizations let teams publish and manage models under a shared
identity. An organization has its own profile page, member list, and
model catalog --- making it easy for teams, companies, or research
groups to establish a recognizable presence on the Hub.

Models published under an organization appear with the organization name
as the author, and visitors can browse all of an organization\'s models
from a single page.

For Developers [​](core_features.html#for-developers){.header-anchor} {#for-developers tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------------------

### Using Models in Your Code [​](core_features.html#using-models-in-your-code){.header-anchor} {#using-models-in-your-code tabindex="-1"}

Every model on the Hub has a **Blob ID** --- a content-addressed
identifier that points to the model\'s files on decentralized storage.
To use a model for inference, you reference it by this Blob ID.

The model\'s \"About\" page shows ready-to-use code snippets for both
the CLI and the Python SDK. A typical workflow looks like:

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import opengradient as og

alpha = og.Alpha(private_key="<key>")

result = alpha.infer(
    model_cid="<blob_id>",
    model_input={"input1": [1.0, 2.0, 3.0]},
    inference_mode=og.InferenceMode.VANILLA
)

print(result.model_output)
print(result.transaction_hash)  # on-chain record of this execution
```
:::

### Markdown Support [​](core_features.html#markdown-support){.header-anchor} {#markdown-support tabindex="-1"}

Model descriptions support most [GitHub Flavored
Markdown](https://www.markdownguide.org/cheat-sheet/) syntax,
[LaTeX](https://ashki23.github.io/markdown-latex.html) for mathematical
notation, and captions for images, tables, and blockquotes.

#### Captions [​](core_features.html#captions){.header-anchor} {#captions tabindex="-1"}

Prefix the caption line with `Caption:` immediately after the element:

::: {.language-markdown .vp-adaptive-theme}
[markdown]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
![Architecture diagram](https://example.com/diagram.png)
Caption: Overview of the model architecture
```
:::

::: {.language-markdown .vp-adaptive-theme}
[markdown]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
| Metric | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Accuracy | 0.94 |
| F1 Score | 0.91 |

Caption: Benchmark results on the test set
```
:::

Note: for tables, leave a blank line between the table and the
`Caption:` line.

</div>
:::
:::

::: {.vp-doc ._api_reference_ style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

OpenGradient API References [​](index.html#opengradient-api-references){.header-anchor} {#opengradient-api-references tabindex="-1"}
=======================================================================================

Comprehensive API documentation for OpenGradient\'s developer tools and
services.

1.  [`OpenGradient Python SDK`](python_sdk/index.html): Python SDK for
    interacting with OpenGradient AI models and infrastructure.
2.  [`MemSync REST API`](https://api.memchat.io/docs): REST API for
    long-term memory management and AI personalization, enabling
    applications to extract, organize, and search memories for
    context-aware interactions.

</div>
:::
:::

::: {.vp-doc ._api_reference_python_sdk_ style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

opengradient

Package opengradient [​](index.html#package-opengradient){.header-anchor} {#package-opengradient tabindex="-1"}
=========================================================================

**Version: 0.8.0**

OpenGradient Python SDK for decentralized AI inference with end-to-end
verification.

Overview [​](index.html#overview){.header-anchor} {#overview tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------

The OpenGradient SDK provides programmatic access to decentralized AI
infrastructure. All LLM inference runs inside Trusted Execution
Environments (TEEs) and settles on-chain via the x402 payment protocol,
giving you cryptographic proof that inference was performed correctly.

The SDK operates across two chains with separate private keys:

-   **[llm](client/llm.html)** (`og.LLM`) \-- LLM chat and completion
    with TEE-verified execution. Pays via x402 on **Base Sepolia**
    (requires OPG tokens).
-   **[alpha](client/alpha.html)** (`og.Alpha`) \-- On-chain ONNX model
    inference with VANILLA, TEE, or ZKML verification. Pays gas on the
    **OpenGradient alpha testnet**.
-   **[model\_hub](client/model_hub.html)** (`og.ModelHub`) \-- Model
    repository management: create, version, and upload ML models.
    Requires email/password auth.
-   **[twins](client/twins.html)** (`og.Twins`) \-- Digital twins chat
    via verifiable inference. Requires a twins API key.

See **`opengradient.types`** for shared data types (`TEE_LLM`,
`InferenceMode`, `TextGenerationOutput`, `x402SettlementMode`, etc.).

LLM Chat [​](index.html#llm-chat){.header-anchor} {#llm-chat tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import asyncio
import opengradient as og

llm = og.LLM(private_key="0x...")

# One-time OPG token approval (idempotent -- skips if allowance is sufficient)
llm.ensure_opg_approval(opg_amount=5)

# Chat with an LLM (TEE-verified)
response = asyncio.run(llm.chat(
    model=og.TEE_LLM.CLAUDE_SONNET_4_6,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello!"}],
    max_tokens=200,
))
print(response.chat_output)
```
:::

Streaming [​](index.html#streaming){.header-anchor} {#streaming tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
async def stream_example():
    llm = og.LLM(private_key="0x...")
    stream = await llm.chat(
        model=og.TEE_LLM.GPT_5,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Explain TEE in one paragraph."}],
        max_tokens=300,
        stream=True,
    )
    async for chunk in stream:
        if chunk.choices[0].delta.content:
            print(chunk.choices[0].delta.content, end="")

asyncio.run(stream_example())
```
:::

On-chain Model Inference [​](index.html#on-chain-model-inference){.header-anchor} {#on-chain-model-inference tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
alpha = og.Alpha(private_key="0x...")
result = alpha.infer(
    model_cid="your_model_cid",
    inference_mode=og.InferenceMode.VANILLA,
    model_input={"input": [1.0, 2.0, 3.0]},
)
print(result.model_output)
```
:::

Model Hub [​](index.html#model-hub){.header-anchor} {#model-hub tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
hub = og.ModelHub(email="you@example.com", password="...")
repo = hub.create_model("my-model", "A price prediction model")
hub.upload("model.onnx", repo.name, repo.initialVersion)
```
:::

</div>

::: {.content v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.content-container v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._api_reference_python_sdk_client_ style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

[opengradient](../index.html) / client

Package opengradient.client [​](index.html#package-opengradient-client){.header-anchor} {#package-opengradient-client tabindex="-1"}
=======================================================================================

OpenGradient Client \-- service modules for the SDK.

Modules [​](index.html#modules){.header-anchor} {#modules tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------

-   **[llm](llm.html)** \-- LLM chat and text completion with
    TEE-verified execution and x402 payment settlement (Base Sepolia OPG
    tokens)
-   **[model\_hub](model_hub.html)** \-- Model repository management:
    create, version, and upload ML models
-   **[alpha](alpha.html)** \-- Alpha Testnet features: on-chain ONNX
    model inference (VANILLA, TEE, ZKML modes), workflow deployment, and
    scheduled ML model execution (OpenGradient testnet gas tokens)
-   **[twins](twins.html)** \-- Digital twins chat via OpenGradient
    verifiable inference

Usage [​](index.html#usage){.header-anchor} {#usage tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import opengradient as og

# LLM inference (Base Sepolia OPG tokens)
llm = og.LLM(private_key="0x...")
llm.ensure_opg_approval(opg_amount=5)
result = await llm.chat(model=og.TEE_LLM.CLAUDE_HAIKU_4_5, messages=[...])

# On-chain model inference (OpenGradient testnet gas tokens)
alpha = og.Alpha(private_key="0x...")
result = alpha.infer(model_cid, og.InferenceMode.VANILLA, model_input)

# Model Hub (requires email auth)
hub = og.ModelHub(email="you@example.com", password="...")
repo = hub.create_model("my-model", "A price prediction model")
```
:::

</div>
:::
:::

::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._api_reference_python_sdk_client_alpha style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

[opengradient](../index.html) / [client](index.html) / alpha

Package opengradient.client.alpha [​](alpha.html#package-opengradient-client-alpha){.header-anchor} {#package-opengradient-client-alpha tabindex="-1"}
===================================================================================================

Alpha Testnet features for OpenGradient SDK.

This module contains features that are only available on the Alpha
Testnet, including on-chain ONNX model inference, workflow management,
and ML model execution.

Classes [​](alpha.html#classes){.header-anchor} {#classes tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------

### `Alpha` [​](alpha.html#alpha){.header-anchor} {#alpha tabindex="-1"}

Alpha Testnet features namespace.

This class provides access to features that are only available on the
Alpha Testnet, including on-chain ONNX model inference, workflow
deployment, and execution.

#### Constructor [​](alpha.html#constructor){.header-anchor} {#constructor tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
def __init__(
    private_key: str,
    rpc_url: str = 'https://ogevmdevnet.opengradient.ai',
    inference_contract_address: str = '0x8383C9bD7462F12Eb996DD02F78234C0421A6FaE',
    api_url: str = 'https://sdk-devnet.opengradient.ai'
)
```
:::

#### Methods [​](alpha.html#methods){.header-anchor} {#methods tabindex="-1"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### `infer()` [​](alpha.html#infer){.header-anchor} {#infer tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
def infer(
    self,
    model_cid: str,
    inference_mode: `InferenceMode`,
    model_input: Dict[str, Union[str, int, float, List, `ndarray`]],
    max_retries: Optional[int] = None
) ‑> `InferenceResult`
```
:::

Perform inference on a model.

**Arguments**

-   **`model_cid (str)`**: The unique content identifier for the model
    from IPFS.
-   **`inference_mode (InferenceMode)`**: The inference mode.
-   **`model_input (Dict[str, Union[str, int, float, List, np.ndarray]])`**:
    The input data for the model.
-   **`max_retries (int, optional)`**: Maximum number of retry attempts.
    Defaults to 5.

**Returns**

InferenceResult (InferenceResult): A dataclass object containing the
transaction hash and model output. transaction\_hash (str): Blockchain
hash for the transaction model\_output (Dict\[str, np.ndarray\]): Output
of the ONNX model

**Raises**

-   **`RuntimeError`**: If the inference fails.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### `new_workflow()` [​](alpha.html#new-workflow){.header-anchor} {#new-workflow tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
def new_workflow(
    self,
    model_cid: str,
    input_query: `HistoricalInputQuery`,
    input_tensor_name: str,
    scheduler_params: Optional[`SchedulerParams`] = None
) ‑> str
```
:::

Deploy a new workflow contract with the specified parameters.

This function deploys a new workflow contract on OpenGradient that
connects an AI model with its required input data. When executed, the
workflow will fetch the specified model, evaluate the input query to get
data, and perform inference.

The workflow can be set to execute manually or automatically via a
scheduler.

**Arguments**

-   **`model_cid (str)`**: CID of the model to be executed from the
    Model Hub
-   **`input_query (HistoricalInputQuery)`**: Input definition for the
    model inference, will be evaluated at runtime for each inference
-   **`input_tensor_name (str)`**: Name of the input tensor expected by
    the model
-   **`scheduler_params (Optional[SchedulerParams])`**: Scheduler
    configuration for automated execution: - frequency: Execution
    frequency in seconds - duration\_hours: How long the schedule should
    live for

**Returns**

str: Deployed contract address. If scheduler\_params was provided, the
workflow will be automatically executed according to the specified
schedule.

**Raises**

-   **`Exception`**: If transaction fails or gas estimation fails

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### `read_workflow_history()` [​](alpha.html#read-workflow-history){.header-anchor} {#read-workflow-history tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
def read_workflow_history(
    self,
    contract_address: str,
    num_results: int
) ‑> List[`ModelOutput`]
```
:::

Gets historical inference results from a workflow contract.

Retrieves the specified number of most recent inference results from the
contract\'s storage, with the most recent result first.

**Arguments**

-   **`contract_address (str)`**: Address of the deployed workflow
    contract
-   **`num_results (int)`**: Number of historical results to retrieve

**Returns**

List\[ModelOutput\]: List of historical inference results

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### `read_workflow_result()` [​](alpha.html#read-workflow-result){.header-anchor} {#read-workflow-result tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
def read_workflow_result(self, contract_address: str) ‑> `ModelOutput`
```
:::

Reads the latest inference result from a deployed workflow contract.

**Arguments**

-   **`contract_address (str)`**: Address of the deployed workflow
    contract

**Returns**

ModelOutput: The inference result from the contract

**Raises**

-   **`ContractLogicError`**: If the transaction fails
-   **`Web3Error`**: If there are issues with the web3 connection or
    contract interaction

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### `run_workflow()` [​](alpha.html#run-workflow){.header-anchor} {#run-workflow tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
def run_workflow(self, contract_address: str) ‑> `ModelOutput`
```
:::

Triggers the run() function on a deployed workflow contract and returns
the result.

**Arguments**

-   **`contract_address (str)`**: Address of the deployed workflow
    contract

**Returns**

ModelOutput: The inference result from the contract

**Raises**

-   **`ContractLogicError`**: If the transaction fails
-   **`Web3Error`**: If there are issues with the web3 connection or
    contract interaction

#### Variables [​](alpha.html#variables){.header-anchor} {#variables tabindex="-1"}

-   `inference_abi` : dict
-   `precompile_abi` : dict

</div>
:::

::: {.vp-doc ._api_reference_python_sdk_client_model_hub style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

[opengradient](../index.html) / [client](index.html) / model\_hub

Package opengradient.client.model\_hub [​](model_hub.html#package-opengradient-client-model-hub){.header-anchor} {#package-opengradient-client-model-hub tabindex="-1"}
================================================================================================================

Model Hub for creating, versioning, and uploading ML models.

Classes [​](model_hub.html#classes){.header-anchor} {#classes tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------

### `ModelHub` [​](model_hub.html#modelhub){.header-anchor} {#modelhub tabindex="-1"}

Model Hub namespace.

Provides access to the OpenGradient Model Hub for creating, versioning,
and uploading ML models. Requires email/password authentication.

#### Constructor [​](model_hub.html#constructor){.header-anchor} {#constructor tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
def __init__(email: Optional[str] = None, password: Optional[str] = None)
```
:::

#### Methods [​](model_hub.html#methods){.header-anchor} {#methods tabindex="-1"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### `create_model()` [​](model_hub.html#create-model){.header-anchor} {#create-model tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
def create_model(
    self,
    model_name: str,
    model_desc: str,
    version: str = '1.00'
) ‑> `ModelRepository`
```
:::

Create a new model with the given model\_name and model\_desc, and a
specified version.

**Arguments**

-   **`model_name (str)`**: The name of the model.
-   **`model_desc (str)`**: The description of the model.
-   **`version (str)`**: The version identifier (default is \"1.00\").

**Returns**

dict: The server response containing model details.

**Raises**

-   **`CreateModelError`**: If the model creation fails.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### `create_version()` [​](model_hub.html#create-version){.header-anchor} {#create-version tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
def create_version(
    self,
    model_name: str,
    notes: str = '',
    is_major: bool = False
) ‑> dict
```
:::

Create a new version for the specified model.

**Arguments**

-   **`model_name (str)`**: The unique identifier for the model.
-   **`notes (str, optional)`**: Notes for the new version.
-   **`is_major (bool, optional)`**: Whether this is a major version
    update. Defaults to False.

**Returns**

dict: The server response containing version details.

**Raises**

-   **`Exception`**: If the version creation fails.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### `list_files()` [​](model_hub.html#list-files){.header-anchor} {#list-files tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
def list_files(self, model_name: str, version: str) ‑> List[Dict]
```
:::

List files for a specific version of a model.

**Arguments**

-   **`model_name (str)`**: The unique identifier for the model.
-   **`version (str)`**: The version identifier for the model.

**Returns**

List\[Dict\]: A list of dictionaries containing file information.

**Raises**

-   **`RuntimeError`**: If the file listing fails.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### `upload()` [​](model_hub.html#upload){.header-anchor} {#upload tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
def upload(
    self,
    model_path: str,
    model_name: str,
    version: str
) ‑> `FileUploadResult`
```
:::

Upload a model file to the server.

**Arguments**

-   **`model_path (str)`**: The path to the model file.
-   **`model_name (str)`**: The unique identifier for the model.
-   **`version (str)`**: The version identifier for the model.

**Returns**

dict: The processed result.

**Raises**

-   **`RuntimeError`**: If the upload fails.

</div>
:::

::: {.vp-doc ._api_reference_python_sdk_client_llm style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

[opengradient](../index.html) / [client](index.html) / llm

Package opengradient.client.llm [​](llm.html#package-opengradient-client-llm){.header-anchor} {#package-opengradient-client-llm tabindex="-1"}
=============================================================================================

LLM chat and completion via TEE-verified execution with x402 payments.

Classes [​](llm.html#classes){.header-anchor} {#classes tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------

### `LLM` [​](llm.html#llm){.header-anchor} {#llm tabindex="-1"}

LLM inference namespace.

Provides access to large language model completions and chat via TEE
(Trusted Execution Environment) with x402 payment protocol support.
Supports both streaming and non-streaming responses.

All request methods (`chat`, `completion`) are async.

Before making LLM requests, ensure your wallet has approved sufficient
OPG tokens for Permit2 spending by calling `ensure_opg_approval`. This
only sends an on-chain transaction when the current allowance is below
the requested amount.

#### Constructor [​](llm.html#constructor){.header-anchor} {#constructor tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
def __init__(
    private_key: str,
    rpc_url: str = 'https://ogevmdevnet.opengradient.ai',
    tee_registry_address: str = '0x4e72238852f3c918f4E4e57AeC9280dDB0c80248',
    llm_server_url: Optional[str] = None
)
```
:::

#### Methods [​](llm.html#methods){.header-anchor} {#methods tabindex="-1"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### `chat()` [​](llm.html#chat){.header-anchor} {#chat tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
async def chat(
    self,
    model: `TEE_LLM`,
    messages: List[Dict],
    max_tokens: int = 100,
    stop_sequence: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temperature: float = 0.0,
    tools: Optional[List[Dict]] = None,
    tool_choice: Optional[str] = None,
    x402_settlement_mode: `x402SettlementMode` = x402SettlementMode.BATCH_HASHED,
    stream: bool = False
) ‑> Union[`TextGenerationOutput`, AsyncGenerator[`StreamChunk`, None]]
```
:::

Perform inference on an LLM model using chat via TEE.

**Arguments**

-   **`model (TEE_LLM)`**: The model to use (e.g.,
    TEE\_LLM.CLAUDE\_HAIKU\_4\_5).
-   **`messages (List[Dict])`**: The messages that will be passed into
    the chat.
-   **`max_tokens (int)`**: Maximum number of tokens for LLM output.
    Default is 100.
-   **`stop_sequence (List[str], optional)`**: List of stop sequences
    for LLM.
-   **`temperature (float)`**: Temperature for LLM inference, between 0
    and 1.
-   **`tools (List[dict], optional)`**: Set of tools for function
    calling.
-   **`tool_choice (str, optional)`**: Sets a specific tool to choose.
-   **`x402_settlement_mode (x402SettlementMode, optional)`**:
    Settlement mode for x402 payments. - PRIVATE: Payment only, no
    input/output data on-chain (most privacy-preserving). -
    BATCH\_HASHED: Aggregates inferences into a Merkle tree with
    input/output hashes and signatures (default, most cost-efficient). -
    INDIVIDUAL\_FULL: Records input, output, timestamp, and verification
    on-chain (maximum auditability). Defaults to BATCH\_HASHED.
-   **`stream (bool, optional)`**: Whether to stream the response.
    Default is False.

**Returns**

Union\[TextGenerationOutput, AsyncGenerator\[StreamChunk, None\]\]: - If
stream=False: TextGenerationOutput with chat\_output, transaction\_hash,
finish\_reason, and payment\_hash - If stream=True: Async generator
yielding StreamChunk objects

**`TextGenerationOutput` fields:**

-   **`transaction_hash`**: Blockchain transaction hash. Set to
    `"external"` for TEE-routed providers.
-   **`finish_reason`**: Reason the model stopped generating (e.g.
    `"stop"`, `"tool_call"`, `"error"`). Only populated for chat
    requests.
-   **`chat_output`**: Dictionary with the assistant message returned by
    a chat request. Contains `role`, `content`, and optionally
    `tool_calls`.
-   **`completion_output`**: Raw text returned by a completion request.
-   **`payment_hash`**: Payment hash for the x402 transaction.
-   **`tee_signature`**: RSA-PSS signature over the response produced by
    the TEE enclave.
-   **`tee_timestamp`**: ISO-8601 timestamp from the TEE at signing
    time.

**Raises**

-   **`RuntimeError`**: If the inference fails.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### `close()` [​](llm.html#close){.header-anchor} {#close tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
async def close(self) ‑> None
```
:::

Close the underlying HTTP client.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### `completion()` [​](llm.html#completion){.header-anchor} {#completion tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
async def completion(
    self,
    model: `TEE_LLM`,
    prompt: str,
    max_tokens: int = 100,
    stop_sequence: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temperature: float = 0.0,
    x402_settlement_mode: `x402SettlementMode` = x402SettlementMode.BATCH_HASHED
) ‑> `TextGenerationOutput`
```
:::

Perform inference on an LLM model using completions via TEE.

**Arguments**

-   **`model (TEE_LLM)`**: The model to use (e.g.,
    TEE\_LLM.CLAUDE\_HAIKU\_4\_5).
-   **`prompt (str)`**: The input prompt for the LLM.
-   **`max_tokens (int)`**: Maximum number of tokens for LLM output.
    Default is 100.
-   **`stop_sequence (List[str], optional)`**: List of stop sequences
    for LLM. Default is None.
-   **`temperature (float)`**: Temperature for LLM inference, between 0
    and 1. Default is 0.0.
-   **`x402_settlement_mode (x402SettlementMode, optional)`**:
    Settlement mode for x402 payments. - PRIVATE: Payment only, no
    input/output data on-chain (most privacy-preserving). -
    BATCH\_HASHED: Aggregates inferences into a Merkle tree with
    input/output hashes and signatures (default, most cost-efficient). -
    INDIVIDUAL\_FULL: Records input, output, timestamp, and verification
    on-chain (maximum auditability). Defaults to BATCH\_HASHED.

**Returns**

TextGenerationOutput: Generated text results including: - Transaction
hash (\"external\" for TEE providers) - String of completion output -
Payment hash for x402 transactions

**`TextGenerationOutput` fields:**

-   **`transaction_hash`**: Blockchain transaction hash. Set to
    `"external"` for TEE-routed providers.
-   **`finish_reason`**: Reason the model stopped generating (e.g.
    `"stop"`, `"tool_call"`, `"error"`). Only populated for chat
    requests.
-   **`chat_output`**: Dictionary with the assistant message returned by
    a chat request. Contains `role`, `content`, and optionally
    `tool_calls`.
-   **`completion_output`**: Raw text returned by a completion request.
-   **`payment_hash`**: Payment hash for the x402 transaction.
-   **`tee_signature`**: RSA-PSS signature over the response produced by
    the TEE enclave.
-   **`tee_timestamp`**: ISO-8601 timestamp from the TEE at signing
    time.

**Raises**

-   **`RuntimeError`**: If the inference fails.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### `ensure_opg_approval()` [​](llm.html#ensure-opg-approval){.header-anchor} {#ensure-opg-approval tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
def ensure_opg_approval(self, opg_amount: float) ‑> [Permit2ApprovalResult](./opg_token)
```
:::

Ensure the Permit2 allowance for OPG is at least `opg_amount`.

Checks the current Permit2 allowance for the wallet. If the allowance is
already \>= the requested amount, returns immediately without sending a
transaction. Otherwise, sends an ERC-20 approve transaction.

**Arguments**

-   **`opg_amount`**: Minimum number of OPG tokens required (e.g. `0.05`
    for 0.05 OPG). Must be at least 0.05 OPG.

**Returns**

Permit2ApprovalResult: Contains `allowance_before`, `allowance_after`,
and `tx_hash` (None when no approval was needed).

**Raises**

-   **`ValueError`**: If the OPG amount is less than 0.05.
-   **`RuntimeError`**: If the approval transaction fails.

</div>
:::
:::

::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._api_reference_python_sdk_client_twins style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

[opengradient](../index.html) / [client](index.html) / twins

Package opengradient.client.twins [​](twins.html#package-opengradient-client-twins){.header-anchor} {#package-opengradient-client-twins tabindex="-1"}
===================================================================================================

Digital twins chat via OpenGradient verifiable inference.

Classes [​](twins.html#classes){.header-anchor} {#classes tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------

### `Twins` [​](twins.html#twins){.header-anchor} {#twins tabindex="-1"}

Digital twins chat namespace.

Provides access to digital twin conversations backed by OpenGradient
verifiable inference. Browse available twins at <https://twin.fun>.

#### Constructor [​](twins.html#constructor){.header-anchor} {#constructor tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
def __init__(api_key: str)
```
:::

#### Methods [​](twins.html#methods){.header-anchor} {#methods tabindex="-1"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### `chat()` [​](twins.html#chat){.header-anchor} {#chat tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
def chat(
    self,
    twin_id: str,
    model: `TEE_LLM`,
    messages: List[Dict],
    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None
) ‑> `TextGenerationOutput`
```
:::

Chat with a digital twin.

**Arguments**

-   **`twin_id`**: The unique identifier of the digital twin.
-   **`model`**: The model to use for inference (e.g.,
    TEE\_LLM.GROK\_4\_1\_FAST\_NON\_REASONING).
-   **`messages`**: The conversation messages to send.
-   **`temperature`**: Sampling temperature. Optional.
-   **`max_tokens`**: Maximum number of tokens for the response.
    Optional.

**Returns**

TextGenerationOutput: Generated text results including chat\_output and
finish\_reason.

**`TextGenerationOutput` fields:**

-   **`transaction_hash`**: Blockchain transaction hash. Set to
    `"external"` for TEE-routed providers.
-   **`finish_reason`**: Reason the model stopped generating (e.g.
    `"stop"`, `"tool_call"`, `"error"`). Only populated for chat
    requests.
-   **`chat_output`**: Dictionary with the assistant message returned by
    a chat request. Contains `role`, `content`, and optionally
    `tool_calls`.
-   **`completion_output`**: Raw text returned by a completion request.
-   **`payment_hash`**: Payment hash for the x402 transaction.
-   **`tee_signature`**: RSA-PSS signature over the response produced by
    the TEE enclave.
-   **`tee_timestamp`**: ISO-8601 timestamp from the TEE at signing
    time.

**Raises**

-   **`RuntimeError`**: If the request fails.

</div>
:::
:::

### Verification & Settlement {#verification-settlement .text v-b7550ba0=""}

::: {.caret role="button" aria-label="toggle section" tabindex="0" v-b7550ba0=""}
[]{.vpi-chevron-right .caret-icon v-b7550ba0=""}

:::

::: {.items v-b7550ba0=""}
::: {.VPSidebarItem .level-2 .is-link v-b7550ba0="" v-b7550ba0=""}
::: {.item v-b7550ba0=""}

[](https://docs.opengradient.ai/learn/onchain_inference/llm_execution.html){.VPLink
.link .link}

Verifiable LLM Execution

::: {.content v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.content-container v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._developers_ style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

Developers Overview [​](index.html#developers-overview){.header-anchor} {#developers-overview tabindex="-1"}
=======================================================================

OpenGradient provides multiple developer tools for building AI-enabled
applications on top of OpenGradient infrastructure, these include the
**OpenGradient Python SDK**, **x402 Integration**, and **MemSync**.

Python SDK [​](index.html#python-sdk){.header-anchor} {#python-sdk tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------

[**Python SDK**](sdk/index.html) enables you to integrate
OpenGradient\'s decentralized, verified AI infrastructure into any
application or agent. The SDK abstracts away all network complexity,
while maintaining full security and decentralization properties.

For LLM inference, the Python SDK provides a convenient wrapper around
the [x402 Gateway](index.html#x402-gateway), allowing you to access
verified LLM inference with a simple Python API instead of managing HTTP
requests and payment flows directly.

The SDK also provides the following features:

::: {.cards}
[**Verifiable LLM Inference**Secure and verifiable large language models
for agentic workflows, executed via x402](sdk/llm.html){.card}[**ML
Inference & Workflows**Run ML model inference and deploy automated
workflows with scheduled execution entirely onchain *(alpha testnet
only)*](sdk/ml_inference.html){.card}[**Model Management**Upload,
manage, and organize models on the Model
Hub](sdk/model_management.html){.card}
:::

::: {.tip .custom-block .github-alert}
TIP

Use the **Python SDK** if you\'re building traditional applications (web
apps, APIs, scripts) that need decentralized AI capabilities.
:::

x402 Gateway [​](index.html#x402-gateway){.header-anchor} {#x402-gateway tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------

[**x402**](https://www.x402.org/) is the open standard for payment-gated
HTTP APIs that OpenGradient uses for LLM inference. Because x402 works
over standard HTTP/REST, any client in any language can access
OpenGradient\'s verified AI infrastructure - no SDK required. Key
properties include:

-   **Universal Access**: Standard HTTP APIs mean any language or
    platform can integrate - JavaScript, Go, Rust, curl, etc.
-   **Payment-Gated Inference**: Cryptographically-verified payments
    using `$OPG` testnet tokens on Base Sepolia
-   **Provable Prompts**: Cryptographic proof of which prompts were
    used, enabling transparent verification of agent actions and AI
    decision-making
-   **TEE Verification**: All LLM inferences are verified using Trusted
    Execution Environments with hardware attestation

Learn more in the [x402 Gateway documentation](x402/index.html).

::: {.tip .custom-block .github-alert}
TIP

Use the **x402 Gateway** directly if you\'re building in a language
other than Python or want fine-grained control over the payment flow.
:::

MemSync [​](index.html#memsync){.header-anchor} {#memsync tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------

[**MemSync**](memsync/index.html) is a long-term memory layer for AI
built on top of OpenGradient\'s verifiable inference and embeddings
infrastructure. It provides a REST API for long-term context management
and AI personalization. Key features include:

-   **Fact Extraction**: Extract and store semantic (long-term) and
    episodic (temporary) facts from conversations
-   **Semantic Search**: Search across personal memories and context
    using semantic similarity
-   **User Profiles**: Manage user profiles and preferences for
    personalized AI experiences
-   **Context Enrichment**: Integrate with external services for
    enhanced context

::: {.tip .custom-block .github-alert}
TIP

Use **MemSync** if you\'re building AI applications that need persistent
memory, user personalization, or long-term context management.
:::

Security & Decentralization [​](index.html#security-decentralization){.header-anchor} {#security-decentralization tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Python SDK and MemSync are powered by the OpenGradient network and
provide the same security guarantees, including:

-   **Decentralization**: No single point of failure
-   **Censorship resistance**: Open access to AI models
-   **Verifiability**: Inferences are cryptographically verified
-   **Transparency**: Full auditability of model execution

</div>
:::
:::

<div>

MemSync [​](index.html#memsync){.header-anchor} {#memsync tabindex="-1"}
===============================================

MemSync is a long-term memory layer for AI built on top of
OpenGradient\'s verifiable inference and embeddings infrastructure. It
provides a REST API for long-term context management and AI
personalization, enabling you to build AI applications that remember
user context and provide personalized experiences.

Overview [​](index.html#overview){.header-anchor} {#overview tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------

MemSync automatically extracts meaningful memories from conversations,
documents, websites, Twitter profiles, and other sources, organizes them
intelligently, and makes them searchable through semantic search. This
allows your AI applications to maintain context across sessions and
provide truly personalized interactions.

Powered by OpenGradient Infrastructure [​](index.html#powered-by-opengradient-infrastructure){.header-anchor} {#powered-by-opengradient-infrastructure tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MemSync is built entirely on OpenGradient\'s [verifiable LLM
inference](https://docs.opengradient.ai/learn/onchain_inference/llm_execution.html)
infrastructure, using TEE-verified execution for all AI processing. This
means every memory operation benefits from OpenGradient\'s cryptographic
guarantees and hardware attestation.

### How MemSync Uses Verifiable Inference [​](index.html#how-memsync-uses-verifiable-inference){.header-anchor} {#how-memsync-uses-verifiable-inference tabindex="-1"}

-   **Memory Extraction**: When you submit conversations, documents, or
    other content, MemSync uses verifiable LLM inference to analyze and
    extract meaningful facts and memories from the input
-   **Memory Classification**: LLM inference determines whether each
    memory is semantic (lasting facts) or episodic (time-bound events),
    ensuring accurate categorization
-   **User Profile Generation**: Auto-generated user bios and insights
    are created through verified LLM processing of stored memories
-   **Memory Maintenance**: Ongoing memory organization, updates, and
    relevance scoring are powered by verifiable inference to maintain
    memory quality over time
-   **Embeddings Generation**: Semantic search capabilities are powered
    by OpenGradient\'s verified embeddings infrastructure for accurate
    memory retrieval

By leveraging OpenGradient\'s x402 infrastructure, MemSync provides:

-   **Integrity Guarantees**: All memory processing is executed in
    TEE-protected environments with hardware attestation
-   **Transparency**: Cryptographic proof of which prompts were used for
    memory operations
-   **Reliability**: Decentralized infrastructure ensures consistent
    availability and performance

Resources [​](index.html#resources){.header-anchor} {#resources tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------

For comprehensive documentation and detailed API reference, see:

-   **[MemSync Guide](https://memsync.mintlify.app/)** - Complete guide
    with detailed examples and advanced features
-   **[API Documentation](https://api.memchat.io/docs)** - Interactive
    API reference with all endpoints and parameters

Key Features [​](index.html#key-features){.header-anchor} {#key-features tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------

-   **Automatic Memory Extraction**: Automatically extracts meaningful
    facts and memories from conversations, documents, websites, Twitter
    profiles, and other sources
-   **Semantic Search**: Search across personal memories using semantic
    similarity for relevant context retrieval
-   **Memory Types**: Intelligently classifies memories as semantic
    (lasting facts) or episodic (time-bound events)
-   **User Profiles**: Auto-generates user profiles with bios and
    insights from stored memories
-   **Memory Categories**: Organizes memories into categories like
    career, interests, relationships, and more
-   **REST API**: Simple REST API for easy integration into any
    application

Quick Start [​](index.html#quick-start){.header-anchor} {#quick-start tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------

### Prerequisites [​](index.html#prerequisites){.header-anchor} {#prerequisites tabindex="-1"}

-   A MemSync account and API key
-   Basic knowledge of REST APIs
-   Your preferred programming language setup (Python, JavaScript, curl,
    etc.)

### Authentication [​](index.html#authentication){.header-anchor} {#authentication tabindex="-1"}

MemSync uses API key-based authentication for all API requests. Include
your API key in the `X-API-Key` header:

::: {.language- .vp-adaptive-theme}
[]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
X-API-Key: YOUR_API_KEY
```
:::

**Note**: OAuth 2.0 authentication will be available soon for enhanced
security.

### Step 1: Store Your First Memory [​](index.html#step-1-store-your-first-memory){.header-anchor} {#step-1-store-your-first-memory tabindex="-1"}

Store a conversation, document, website content, Twitter profile, or
other source that MemSync will process to extract meaningful memories:

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import requests

url = "https://api.memchat.io/v1/memories"
headers = {
    "X-API-Key": "YOUR_API_KEY",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
}

data = {
    "messages": [
        {
            "role": "user", 
            "content": "I'm a software engineer at Google working on machine learning projects. I love hiking and photography in my free time."
        },
        {
            "role": "assistant", 
            "content": "That's great! It sounds like you have a nice balance between your technical work and creative hobbies."
        }
    ],
    "agent_id": "my-chatbot",
    "thread_id": "conversation-123",
    "source": "chat"
}

response = requests.post(url, json=data, headers=headers)
print(response.json())
```
:::

### Step 2: Search for Relevant Memories [​](index.html#step-2-search-for-relevant-memories){.header-anchor} {#step-2-search-for-relevant-memories tabindex="-1"}

Search for memories related to user interests:

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import requests

url = "https://api.memchat.io/v1/memories/search"
headers = {
    "X-API-Key": "YOUR_API_KEY",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
}

data = {
    "query": "What does the user do for work?",
    "limit": 5,
    "rerank": True
}

response = requests.post(url, json=data, headers=headers)
result = response.json()

print("User Bio:", result['user_bio'])
print("Relevant Memories:")
for memory in result['memories']:
    print(f"- {memory['memory']}")
```
:::

### Step 3: Get User Profile [​](index.html#step-3-get-user-profile){.header-anchor} {#step-3-get-user-profile tabindex="-1"}

Retrieve the user\'s complete profile with auto-generated bio and
insights:

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import requests

url = "https://api.memchat.io/v1/users/profile"
headers = {
    "X-API-Key": "YOUR_API_KEY"
}

response = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
profile = response.json()

print("User Bio:", profile['user_bio'])
print("Profiles:", profile['profiles'])
print("Insights:", profile['insights'])
```
:::

Memory Types [​](index.html#memory-types){.header-anchor} {#memory-types tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------

MemSync uses two distinct types of memories to capture different kinds
of information:

### Semantic Memories [​](index.html#semantic-memories){.header-anchor} {#semantic-memories tabindex="-1"}

**Semantic memories** represent stable, lasting facts that are not tied
to a specific time or place. These are core truths about a user that
remain relatively constant over time.

**Characteristics:**

-   **Persistent**: Don\'t change frequently
-   **Context-independent**: True regardless of when or where they were
    mentioned
-   **Factual**: Represent established knowledge about the user
-   **Searchable**: Highly valuable for providing relevant context

**Examples:**

-   Identity: \"User is a software engineer at Google\"
-   Skills: \"Experienced in Python and machine learning\"
-   Preferences: \"Prefers working in collaborative environments\"
-   Interests: \"Passionate about hiking and photography\"

### Episodic Memories [​](index.html#episodic-memories){.header-anchor} {#episodic-memories tabindex="-1"}

**Episodic memories** relate to current situations, goals, or projects
that might change over time. They are tied to specific moments and could
evolve or become outdated.

**Characteristics:**

-   **Time-bound**: Relevant to a specific period or context
-   **Dynamic**: May change, complete, or become irrelevant over time
-   **Situational**: Tied to particular circumstances or goals
-   **Contextual**: Provide important background for current
    interactions

**Examples:**

-   Current Projects: \"Currently working on a new iOS app\"
-   Active Goals: \"Learning how to cook new recipes\"
-   Recent Events: \"Just moved to a new apartment last week\"
-   Temporary States: \"Currently traveling in Europe for 2 weeks\"

### Memory Classification [​](index.html#memory-classification){.header-anchor} {#memory-classification tabindex="-1"}

MemSync automatically classifies memories during extraction using
advanced language models. The system analyzes:

-   Temporal indicators (words like \"currently\", \"recently\",
    \"always\")
-   Context and content analysis
-   Conversation patterns

Understanding Search Results [​](index.html#understanding-search-results){.header-anchor} {#understanding-search-results tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When you search for memories, MemSync returns:

::: {.language-json .vp-adaptive-theme}
[json]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
{
  "user_bio": "Software engineer at Google specializing in machine learning with interests in hiking and photography",
  "memories": [
    {
      "id": "mem_123",
      "memory": "Works as a software engineer at Google focusing on machine learning projects",
      "categories": ["career"],
      "type": "semantic",
      "vector_distance": 0.15,
      "rerank_score": 0.92,
      "source": "chat",
      "created_at": "2024-03-20T10:00:00Z"
    }
  ]
}
```
:::

**Key Fields:**

-   **user\_bio**: Auto-generated summary of the user
-   **memory**: The extracted fact or memory
-   **categories**: Organized tags (career, interests, etc.)
-   **type**: `semantic` (lasting facts) or `episodic` (time-bound
    events)
-   **vector\_distance**: Similarity score for search relevance
-   **rerank\_score**: Enhanced relevance score when reranking is
    enabled

Use Cases [​](index.html#use-cases){.header-anchor} {#use-cases tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------

MemSync is ideal for building:

-   **Personalized Chatbots**: Chatbots that remember user preferences
    and context across conversations
-   **AI Assistants**: Virtual assistants that learn about users over
    time
-   **Context-Aware Applications**: Applications that adapt based on
    user history and preferences
-   **Memory-Enhanced LLM Apps**: LLM applications that maintain
    long-term context beyond token limits

Best Practices [​](index.html#best-practices){.header-anchor} {#best-practices tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------------

### Memory Extraction [​](index.html#memory-extraction){.header-anchor} {#memory-extraction tabindex="-1"}

-   Provide rich conversation context to help MemSync understand what
    information is important
-   Include relevant details that help classify memories correctly
-   Use consistent terminology for better memory organization

### Search Strategy [​](index.html#search-strategy){.header-anchor} {#search-strategy tabindex="-1"}

-   Use broad queries to find semantic memories (e.g., \"What does the
    user do for work?\")
-   Use specific queries to find episodic memories (e.g., \"What is the
    user currently working on?\")
-   Combine both types in search results for comprehensive context
-   Enable reranking for better relevance when needed

### Memory Lifecycle [​](index.html#memory-lifecycle){.header-anchor} {#memory-lifecycle tabindex="-1"}

-   Semantic memories typically have longer relevance
-   Episodic memories may need periodic updates or cleanup
-   Monitor memory relevance over time and update as needed

API Base URL [​](index.html#api-base-url){.header-anchor} {#api-base-url tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------

All MemSync API endpoints are available at:

::: {.language- .vp-adaptive-theme}
[]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
https://api.memchat.io/v1
```
:::

Common Issues [​](index.html#common-issues){.header-anchor} {#common-issues tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------

**401 Unauthorized Error**: Make sure your API key is valid and included
in the `X-API-Key` header.

**No memories returned**: It may take a few seconds for memories to be
processed and indexed. Try searching again, or check that your
conversation contained meaningful information.

**Rate limiting**: MemSync has rate limits to ensure service quality. If
you hit limits, implement exponential backoff in your retry logic.

Try It Out [​](index.html#try-it-out){.header-anchor} {#try-it-out tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------

You can see MemSync in action by trying out the
[memsync.ai](https://memsync.ai) app. The MemSync app showcases how
memory extraction, semantic search, and user profiles work in practice,
giving you a hands-on experience with the MemSync capabilities.

</div>
:::
