<div>

MemSync Tutorial [​](tutorial.html#memsync-tutorial){.header-anchor} {#memsync-tutorial tabindex="-1"}
====================================================================

::: {.note .custom-block .github-alert}
NOTE

For comprehensive documentation and detailed examples, see the [Full
Guide](https://memsync.mintlify.app/).
:::

Welcome to MemSync [​](tutorial.html#welcome-to-memsync){.header-anchor} {#welcome-to-memsync tabindex="-1"}
------------------------------------------------------------------------

This quickstart guide will help you integrate MemSync into your
application and start building personalized AI experiences. You\'ll
learn how to store memories, search for relevant context, and retrieve
user profiles.

Prerequisites [​](tutorial.html#prerequisites){.header-anchor} {#prerequisites tabindex="-1"}
--------------------------------------------------------------

Before getting started, make sure you have:

-   A MemSync account and API key
-   Basic knowledge of REST APIs
-   Your preferred programming language setup (Python, JavaScript, etc.)

Authentication [​](tutorial.html#authentication){.header-anchor} {#authentication tabindex="-1"}
----------------------------------------------------------------

In order to interact with the MemSync API you need to create a service
account and API key. You can register and generate an API key in the
[MemSync](https://memsync.ai) app.

::: {.tip .custom-block .github-alert}
TIP

You can generate API keys on the [MemSync
Developers](https://app.memsync.ai/dashboard/api-keys) page.
:::

MemSync uses API key-based authentication for all API requests. You\'ll
need to include your API key in the `X-API-Key` header:

::: {.language- .vp-adaptive-theme}
[]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
X-API-Key: YOUR_API_KEY
```
:::

**Coming Soon:** OAuth 2.0 authentication will be available for enhanced
security and easier integration with third-party applications.

Step 1: Store Your First Memory [​](tutorial.html#step-1-store-your-first-memory){.header-anchor} {#step-1-store-your-first-memory tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Let\'s start by storing a conversation that MemSync will process to
extract meaningful memories:

::: {.vp-code-group .vp-adaptive-theme}
::: {.tabs}
pythonjavascript
:::

::: {.blocks}
::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme .active}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import requests

url = "https://api.memchat.io/v1/memories"
headers = {
    "X-API-Key": "YOUR_API_KEY",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
}

data = {
    "messages": [
        {
            "role": "user", 
            "content": "I'm a software engineer at Google working on machine learning projects. I love hiking and photography in my free time."
        },
        {
            "role": "assistant", 
            "content": "That's great! It sounds like you have a nice balance between your technical work and creative hobbies."
        }
    ],
    "agent_id": "my-chatbot",
    "thread_id": "conversation-123",
    "source": "chat"
}

response = requests.post(url, json=data, headers=headers)
print(response.json())
```
:::

::: {.language-javascript .vp-adaptive-theme}
[javascript]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
const response = await fetch("https://api.memchat.io/v1/memories", {
  method: "POST",
  headers: {
    "X-API-Key": "YOUR_API_KEY",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
  },
  body: JSON.stringify({
    messages: [
      {
        role: "user",
        content: "I'm a software engineer at Google working on machine learning projects. I love hiking and photography in my free time."
      },
      {
        role: "assistant",
        content: "That's great! It sounds like you have a nice balance between your technical work and creative hobbies."
      }
    ],
    agent_id: "my-chatbot",
    thread_id: "conversation-123",
    source: "chat"
  })
});

const data = await response.json();
console.log(data);
```
:::
:::
:::

Step 2: Search for Relevant Memories [​](tutorial.html#step-2-search-for-relevant-memories){.header-anchor} {#step-2-search-for-relevant-memories tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Now let\'s search for memories related to the user\'s interests:

::: {.vp-code-group .vp-adaptive-theme}
::: {.tabs}
pythonjavascript
:::

::: {.blocks}
::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme .active}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import requests

url = "https://api.memchat.io/v1/memories/search"
headers = {
    "X-API-Key": "YOUR_API_KEY",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
}

data = {
    "query": "What does the user do for work?",
    "limit": 5,
    "rerank": True
}

response = requests.post(url, json=data, headers=headers)
result = response.json()

print("User Bio:", result['user_bio'])
print("Relevant Memories:")
for memory in result['memories']:
    print(f"- {memory['memory']}")
```
:::

::: {.language-javascript .vp-adaptive-theme}
[javascript]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
const response = await fetch("https://api.memchat.io/v1/memories/search", {
  method: "POST",
  headers: {
    "X-API-Key": "YOUR_API_KEY",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
  },
  body: JSON.stringify({
    query: "What does the user do for work?",
    limit: 5,
    rerank: true
  })
});

const result = await response.json();
console.log("User Bio:", result.user_bio);
console.log("Relevant Memories:");
result.memories.forEach(memory => {
  console.log(`- ${memory.memory}`);
});
```
:::
:::
:::

Step 3: Get User Profile [​](tutorial.html#step-3-get-user-profile){.header-anchor} {#step-3-get-user-profile tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Retrieve the user\'s complete profile with auto-generated bio and
insights:

::: {.vp-code-group .vp-adaptive-theme}
::: {.tabs}
pythonjavascript
:::

::: {.blocks}
::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme .active}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import requests

url = "https://api.memchat.io/v1/users/profile"
headers = {
    "X-API-Key": "YOUR_API_KEY"
}

response = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
profile = response.json()

print("User Bio:", profile['user_bio'])
print("Profiles:", profile['profiles'])
print("Insights:", profile['insights'])
```
:::

::: {.language-javascript .vp-adaptive-theme}
[javascript]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
const response = await fetch("https://api.memchat.io/v1/users/profile", {
  method: "GET",
  headers: {
    "X-API-Key": "YOUR_API_KEY"
  }
});

const profile = await response.json();
console.log("User Bio:", profile.user_bio);
console.log("Profiles:", profile.profiles);
console.log("Insights:", profile.insights);
```
:::
:::
:::

Understanding the Response [​](tutorial.html#understanding-the-response){.header-anchor} {#understanding-the-response tabindex="-1"}
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When you search for memories, MemSync returns:

::: {.language-json .vp-adaptive-theme}
[json]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
{
  "user_bio": "Software engineer at Google specializing in machine learning with interests in hiking and photography",
  "memories": [
    {
      "id": "mem_123",
      "memory": "Works as a software engineer at Google focusing on machine learning projects",
      "categories": ["career"],
      "type": "semantic",
      "vector_distance": 0.15,
      "rerank_score": 0.92,
      "source": "chat",
      "created_at": "2024-03-20T10:00:00Z"
    }
  ]
}
```
:::

### Key Fields: [​](tutorial.html#key-fields){.header-anchor} {#key-fields tabindex="-1"}

-   **user\_bio**: Auto-generated summary of the user
-   **memory**: The extracted fact or memory
-   **categories**: Organized tags (career, interests, etc.)
-   **type**: `semantic` (lasting facts) or `episodic` (time-bound
    events)
-   **vector\_distance**: Similarity score for search relevance
-   **rerank\_score**: Enhanced relevance score when reranking is
    enabled

Next Steps [​](tutorial.html#next-steps){.header-anchor} {#next-steps tabindex="-1"}
--------------------------------------------------------

Now that you\'ve made your first API calls, explore these advanced
features:

-   **Memory Categories**: Learn how memories are organized into
    categories like work, hobbies, and relationships
-   **Integration Setup**: Import data from social media, documents, and
    chat platforms
-   **API Reference**: Explore all available endpoints and advanced
    features
-   **Best Practices**: Learn optimization techniques for memory storage
    and retrieval

Example: Building a Personalized Chatbot [​](tutorial.html#example-building-a-personalized-chatbot){.header-anchor} {#example-building-a-personalized-chatbot tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Here\'s a complete example of how to build a chatbot that remembers user
context:

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import requests

class PersonalizedChatbot:
    def __init__(self, api_key):
        self.api_key = api_key
        self.base_url = "https://api.memchat.io/v1"
        self.headers = {
            "X-API-Key": api_key,
            "Content-Type": "application/json"
        }
    
    def chat(self, user_message, user_id, thread_id="default"):
        # 1. Search for relevant memories
        memories = self.search_memories(user_message)
        
        # 2. Create context from memories
        context = self.build_context(memories)
        
        # 3. Generate response (using your LLM)
        response = self.generate_response(context, user_message)
        
        # 4. Store the conversation
        self.store_conversation(user_message, response, thread_id)
        
        return response
    
    def search_memories(self, query):
        response = requests.post(
            f"{self.base_url}/memories/search",
            headers=self.headers,
            json={"query": query, "limit": 5, "rerank": True}
        )
        return response.json()
    
    def store_conversation(self, user_msg, bot_response, thread_id):
        requests.post(
            f"{self.base_url}/memories",
            headers=self.headers,
            json={
                "messages": [
                    {"role": "user", "content": user_msg},
                    {"role": "assistant", "content": bot_response}
                ],
                "agent_id": "my-chatbot",
                "thread_id": thread_id,
                "source": "chat"
            }
        )

# Usage
chatbot = PersonalizedChatbot("YOUR_API_KEY")
response = chatbot.chat("What should I work on today?", "user123")
```
:::

This example shows the basic pattern: search for context, generate
response, store conversation. MemSync handles the complex memory
extraction and organization automatically.

Common Issues [​](tutorial.html#common-issues){.header-anchor} {#common-issues tabindex="-1"}
--------------------------------------------------------------

### 401 Unauthorized Error [​](tutorial.html#_401-unauthorized-error){.header-anchor} {#_401-unauthorized-error tabindex="-1"}

Make sure your API key is correctly set in the `X-API-Key` header and
that it\'s valid.

### No memories returned [​](tutorial.html#no-memories-returned){.header-anchor} {#no-memories-returned tabindex="-1"}

Ensure you\'ve stored some conversations first using the `/memories`
endpoint before searching.

### Rate limiting [​](tutorial.html#rate-limiting){.header-anchor} {#rate-limiting tabindex="-1"}

MemSync has rate limits to ensure fair usage. If you hit rate limits,
implement exponential backoff and retry logic.

::: {.tip .custom-block .github-alert}
TIP

Ready to dive deeper? Check out our [Full
Guide](https://memsync.mintlify.app/), Core Concepts, or explore the
full API reference.
:::

</div>

::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._developers_x402_ style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

x402 Gateway [​](index.html#x402-gateway){.header-anchor} {#x402-gateway tabindex="-1"}
=========================================================

The x402 Gateway provides direct HTTP access to OpenGradient\'s TEE
verified LLM inference without requiring the Python SDK. Because x402 is
a standard HTTP protocol, you can integrate from any language or
platform - JavaScript, Go, Rust, Python, curl, or any HTTP client.

What is x402? [​](index.html#what-is-x402){.header-anchor} {#what-is-x402 tabindex="-1"}
----------------------------------------------------------

[x402](https://www.x402.org/) is an open standard that extends HTTP with
payment requirements using the `402 Payment Required` status code. When
you make a request to the x402 Gateway:

1.  The server responds with payment requirements
2.  You sign a payment authorization
3.  You resubmit the request with the signed payment
4.  The inference executes, payment settles on Base Sepolia, and proofs
    settle on the OpenGradient network

All LLM inferences are verified using TEE (Trusted Execution
Environments), providing cryptographic proof of execution.

::: {.tip .custom-block .github-alert}
TIP

For a deep dive into how x402 works on OpenGradient, including TEE
verification and settlement modes, see [LLM
Execution](https://docs.opengradient.ai/learn/onchain_inference/llm_execution.html).
:::

Quick Start [​](index.html#quick-start){.header-anchor} {#quick-start tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------

### Prerequisites [​](index.html#prerequisites){.header-anchor} {#prerequisites tabindex="-1"}

-   A wallet with a private key (such as MetaMask)
-   `$OPG` testnet tokens on [Base
    Sepolia](../../learn/network/deployment.html) (get tokens from the
    [faucet](https://faucet.opengradient.ai))

### Basic Flow [​](index.html#basic-flow){.header-anchor} {#basic-flow tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-bash .vp-adaptive-theme}
[bash]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
# 1. Make initial request - get payment requirements
curl -X POST https://llm.opengradient.ai/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "model": "openai/gpt-4o",
    "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello!"}],
    "max_tokens": 100
  }'

# Response: 402 Payment Required with payment details in headers
```
:::

The server returns a `402` status with payment requirements. You then:

1.  Parse the payment requirements from the response headers
2.  Create and sign a payment payload with your wallet
3.  Resubmit the request with the `X-PAYMENT` header containing your
    signed payment

::: {.tip .custom-block .github-alert}
TIP

You can find x402 client libraries to automate these steps in
Coinbase\'s [x402 repo](https://github.com/coinbase/x402).
:::

See [Examples](examples.html) for complete implementations in multiple
languages.

Endpoint [​](index.html#endpoint){.header-anchor} {#endpoint tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------

  Environment   URL
  ------------- -------------------------------
  Production    `https://llm.opengradient.ai`

Supported Models [​](index.html#supported-models){.header-anchor} {#supported-models tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------------

  Model ID                             Provider
  ------------------------------------ -----------
  `openai/gpt-4.1-2025-04-14`          OpenAI
  `openai/gpt-4o`                      OpenAI
  `openai/o4-mini`                     OpenAI
  `anthropic/claude-4.0-sonnet`        Anthropic
  `anthropic/claude-3.7-sonnet`        Anthropic
  `anthropic/claude-3.5-haiku`         Anthropic
  `google/gemini-2.5-flash`            Google
  `google/gemini-2.5-pro`              Google
  `google/gemini-2.5-flash-lite`       Google
  `google/gemini-2.0-flash`            Google
  `x-ai/grok-3-beta`                   xAI
  `x-ai/grok-3-mini-beta`              xAI
  `x-ai/grok-4.1-fast`                 xAI
  `x-ai/grok-4-1-fast-non-reasoning`   xAI
  `x-ai/grok-2-1212`                   xAI
  `x-ai/grok-2-vision-latest`          xAI

Payment Details [​](index.html#payment-details){.header-anchor} {#payment-details tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------------

x402 LLM inference is paid for using `$OPG` testnet tokens on **Base
Sepolia**. All other operations --- TEE node registration, inference
execution, proof settlement, and verification --- happen on the
**OpenGradient network**.

  Property           Value
  ------------------ -------------------------------------------------------
  Payment Network    Base Sepolia
  Token              `$OPG` (`0x240b09731D96979f50B2C649C9CE10FcF9C7987F`)
  Chain ID           84532
  Proof Settlement   OpenGradient Network

Payments are settled on Base Sepolia after inference execution.
Inference proofs are settled separately on the OpenGradient network. You
can get `$OPG` testnet tokens from the
[faucet](https://faucet.opengradient.ai).

Settlement Modes [​](index.html#settlement-modes){.header-anchor} {#settlement-modes tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------------

Control how inference data is recorded on the OpenGradient blockchain by
setting the `X-SETTLEMENT-TYPE` header:

  Mode         Header Value   Description
  ------------ -------------- -----------------------------------------------------
  Private      `private`      No on-chain data, most private
  Individual   `individual`   Input/output hashes on-chain
  Batch        `batch`        Aggregated hashes for multiple inferences (default)

::: {.note .custom-block .github-alert}
NOTE

The SDK uses `og.x402SettlementMode.PRIVATE`,
`og.x402SettlementMode.INDIVIDUAL_FULL`, and
`og.x402SettlementMode.BATCH_HASHED` as aliases for these values.
:::

Use `individual` (or `og.x402SettlementMode.INDIVIDUAL_FULL` in the SDK)
when you need to prove which prompts were used for agent actions or
decision verification.

When to Use x402 vs SDK [​](index.html#when-to-use-x402-vs-sdk){.header-anchor} {#when-to-use-x402-vs-sdk tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  Use x402 directly when\...                      Use Python SDK when\...
  ----------------------------------------------- -------------------------------------
  Building in JavaScript, Go, Rust, etc.          Building in Python
  You want fine-grained control over payments     You want automatic payment handling
  Integrating into existing HTTP infrastructure   Starting a new Python project
  Building a custom SDK for another language      Prototyping quickly

Next Steps [​](index.html#next-steps){.header-anchor} {#next-steps tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------

-   [API Reference](api-reference.html) - Complete HTTP API
    documentation
-   [Examples](examples.html) - Integration examples in multiple
    languages
-   [LLM
    Execution](https://docs.opengradient.ai/learn/onchain_inference/llm_execution.html) -
    How x402 and TEE verification work

</div>
:::
:::

::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._developers_x402_examples style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

Integration Examples [​](examples.html#integration-examples){.header-anchor} {#integration-examples tabindex="-1"}
============================================================================

Complete examples for integrating with the x402 Gateway using the
official [x402 client libraries](https://github.com/coinbase/x402).

TypeScript / JavaScript [​](examples.html#typescript-javascript){.header-anchor} {#typescript-javascript tabindex="-1"}
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use the `@x402/fetch` package, which wraps the standard `fetch` API with
automatic payment handling.

::: {.language-bash .vp-adaptive-theme}
[bash]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
npm install @x402/fetch @x402/evm viem
```
:::

::: {.language-typescript .vp-adaptive-theme}
[typescript]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import { wrapFetch } from "@x402/fetch";
import { ExactEvmScheme } from "@x402/evm/exact/client";
import { createWalletClient, http } from "viem";
import { privateKeyToAccount } from "viem/accounts";

// Create wallet client (payments use $OPG tokens on Base Sepolia)
const account = privateKeyToAccount(process.env.PRIVATE_KEY as `0x${string}`);
const walletClient = createWalletClient({
  account,
  chain: {
    id: 84532,
    name: "Base Sepolia",
    nativeCurrency: { name: "ETH", symbol: "ETH", decimals: 18 },
    rpcUrls: { default: { http: ["https://sepolia.base.org"] } },
  },
  transport: http(),
});

// Wrap fetch with x402 payment handling
const x402Fetch = wrapFetch(fetch, {
  schemes: [
    { network: "eip155:84532", client: new ExactEvmScheme(walletClient) },
  ],
});

// Use like normal fetch - payments handled automatically
const response = await x402Fetch(
  "https://llm.opengradient.ai/v1/chat/completions",
  {
    method: "POST",
    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    body: JSON.stringify({
      model: "openai/gpt-4o",
      messages: [{ role: "user", content: "What is quantum computing?" }],
      max_tokens: 200,
    }),
  }
);

const result = await response.json();
console.log(result.choices[0].message.content);
```
:::

For other HTTP clients, see
[\@x402/axios](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@x402/axios) or
[\@x402/core](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@x402/core) for lower-level
control.

Python [​](examples.html#python){.header-anchor} {#python tabindex="-1"}
------------------------------------------------

Use the [OpenGradient
SDK](https://docs.opengradient.ai/developers/sdk/llm.html) which handles
x402 payments automatically:

::: {.language-bash .vp-adaptive-theme}
[bash]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
pip install opengradient
```
:::

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import asyncio
import opengradient as og
import os

llm = og.LLM(
    private_key=os.environ.get("OG_PRIVATE_KEY"),
)

llm.ensure_opg_approval(opg_amount=5.0)

result = asyncio.run(llm.chat(
    model=og.TEE_LLM.GPT_5,
    messages=[
        {"role": "user", "content": "What is quantum computing?"}
    ],
    max_tokens=200
))

print(result.chat_output["content"])
print(f"Payment hash: {result.payment_hash}")
```
:::

See the [SDK
documentation](https://docs.opengradient.ai/developers/sdk/llm.html) for
more examples including tool calling and TEE inference.

Go [​](examples.html#go){.header-anchor} {#go tabindex="-1"}
----------------------------------------

Use the official x402 Go client library for automatic payment handling.

::: {.language-bash .vp-adaptive-theme}
[bash]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
go get github.com/coinbase/x402/go
```
:::

::: {.language-go .vp-adaptive-theme}
[go]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
package main

import (
   "bytes"
   "encoding/json"
   "fmt"
   "io"
   "net/http"
   "os"

   x402 "github.com/coinbase/x402/go"
   x402http "github.com/coinbase/x402/go/http"
   evm "github.com/coinbase/x402/go/mechanisms/evm/exact/client"
   evmsigners "github.com/coinbase/x402/go/signers/evm"
)

type Message struct {
   Role    string `json:"role"`
   Content string `json:"content"`
}

type ChatRequest struct {
   Model     string    `json:"model"`
   Messages  []Message `json:"messages"`
   MaxTokens int       `json:"max_tokens"`
}

func main() {
   // Create signer from private key
   signer, err := evmsigners.NewClientSignerFromPrivateKey(os.Getenv("PRIVATE_KEY"))
   if err != nil {
       fmt.Println("Error creating signer:", err)
       return
   }

   // Create x402 client and register Base Sepolia for $OPG payments
   client := x402.Newx402Client().
       Register("eip155:84532", evm.NewExactEvmScheme(signer))

   // Wrap HTTP client with automatic payment handling
   httpClient := x402http.WrapHTTPClientWithPayment(
       http.DefaultClient,
       x402http.Newx402HTTPClient(client),
   )

   // Prepare request body
   body, _ := json.Marshal(ChatRequest{
       Model: "openai/gpt-4o",
       Messages: []Message{
           {Role: "user", Content: "What is quantum computing?"},
       },
       MaxTokens: 200,
   })

   // Make request - payments handled automatically
   resp, err := httpClient.Post(
       "https://llm.opengradient.ai/v1/chat/completions",
       "application/json",
       bytes.NewReader(body),
   )
   if err != nil {
       fmt.Println("Error:", err)
       return
   }
   defer resp.Body.Close()

   // Parse response
   respBody, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
   var result map[string]interface{}
   json.Unmarshal(respBody, &result)

   choices := result["choices"].([]interface{})
   message := choices[0].(map[string]interface{})["message"].(map[string]interface{})
   fmt.Println(message["content"])
}
```
:::

See the [x402 Go
documentation](https://github.com/coinbase/x402/tree/main/go) for more
details.

curl (Testing) [​](examples.html#curl-testing){.header-anchor} {#curl-testing tabindex="-1"}
--------------------------------------------------------------

For testing and debugging, you can use curl to inspect the payment flow:

::: {.language-bash .vp-adaptive-theme}
[bash]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
# Step 1: Get payment requirements
curl -X POST https://llm.opengradient.ai/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "model": "openai/gpt-4o",
    "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello!"}],
    "max_tokens": 100
  }' \
  -i

# The response will include X-PAYMENT-REQUIRED header
# Decode it with: echo "<base64>" | base64 -d | jq
```
:::

::: {.note .custom-block .github-alert}
NOTE

The payment signing step requires cryptographic operations that can\'t
be done in pure bash. Use one of the language examples above for the
signing step.
:::

With Settlement Mode [​](examples.html#with-settlement-mode){.header-anchor} {#with-settlement-mode tabindex="-1"}
----------------------------------------------------------------------------

To use a specific settlement mode, add the `X-SETTLEMENT-TYPE` header:

::: {.language-typescript .vp-adaptive-theme}
[typescript]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
// TypeScript example with metadata settlement
const response = await x402Fetch(
  "https://llm.opengradient.ai/v1/chat/completions",
  {
    method: "POST",
    headers: {
      "Content-Type": "application/json",
      "X-SETTLEMENT-TYPE": "individual", // Full prompt/response on-chain
    },
    body: JSON.stringify({
      model: "openai/gpt-4o",
      messages: [{ role: "user", content: "Hello!" }],
      max_tokens: 100,
    }),
  }
);
```
:::

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
# Python SDK example with full individual settlement
result = await llm.chat(
    model=og.TEE_LLM.GPT_4_1_2025_04_14,
    messages=messages,
    x402_settlement_mode=og.x402SettlementMode.INDIVIDUAL_FULL
)
```
:::

x402 Client Libraries [​](examples.html#x402-client-libraries){.header-anchor} {#x402-client-libraries tabindex="-1"}
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The official x402 libraries handle all payment complexity for you:

  Package                                                     Description
  ----------------------------------------------------------- --------------------------------------------------
  [\@x402/core](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@x402/core)     Core x402 client/server implementation
  [\@x402/evm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@x402/evm)       EVM chain support (Ethereum, Base, OpenGradient)
  [\@x402/fetch](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@x402/fetch)   Fetch API wrapper
  [\@x402/axios](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@x402/axios)   Axios interceptor

See the [x402 GitHub repository](https://github.com/coinbase/x402) for
more examples and documentation.

Next Steps [​](examples.html#next-steps){.header-anchor} {#next-steps tabindex="-1"}
--------------------------------------------------------

-   [API Reference](api-reference.html) - Complete endpoint
    documentation
-   [Python SDK](https://docs.opengradient.ai/developers/sdk/llm.html) -
    Simplified Python integration
-   [LLM
    Execution](https://docs.opengradient.ai/learn/onchain_inference/llm_execution.html) -
    How x402 works under the hood

</div>
:::
:::

::: {.vp-doc ._developers_x402_api-reference style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

API Reference [​](api-reference.html#api-reference){.header-anchor} {#api-reference tabindex="-1"}
===================================================================

Complete HTTP API documentation for the x402 Gateway.

Base URL [​](api-reference.html#base-url){.header-anchor} {#base-url tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------

::: {.language- .vp-adaptive-theme}
[]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
https://llm.opengradient.ai
```
:::

Authentication Flow [​](api-reference.html#authentication-flow){.header-anchor} {#authentication-flow tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The x402 protocol uses a two-step authentication flow:

1.  **Initial Request**: Make a request without payment headers
2.  **Payment Response**: Server responds with `402 Payment Required`
    and payment details
3.  **Authenticated Request**: Resubmit with signed payment in
    `X-PAYMENT` header

Endpoints [​](api-reference.html#endpoints){.header-anchor} {#endpoints tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------

### Chat Completions [​](api-reference.html#chat-completions){.header-anchor} {#chat-completions tabindex="-1"}

Generate chat completions with conversational context.

::: {.language-http .vp-adaptive-theme}
[http]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
POST /v1/chat/completions
```
:::

#### Request Headers [​](api-reference.html#request-headers){.header-anchor} {#request-headers tabindex="-1"}

  Header                Required      Description
  --------------------- ------------- ------------------------------------------------------
  `Content-Type`        Yes           Must be `application/json`
  `X-PAYMENT`           Conditional   Signed payment payload (required after 402 response)
  `X-SETTLEMENT-TYPE`   No            Settlement mode: `private`, `individual`, or `batch`

#### Request Body [​](api-reference.html#request-body){.header-anchor} {#request-body tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-json .vp-adaptive-theme}
[json]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
{
  "model": "openai/gpt-4o",
  "messages": [
    {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful assistant."},
    {"role": "user", "content": "Hello!"}
  ],
  "max_tokens": 100,
  "temperature": 0.7,
  "stop": ["\n"],
  "tools": [],
  "tool_choice": "auto"
}
```
:::

  Field           Type      Required   Description
  --------------- --------- ---------- ------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `model`         string    Yes        Model identifier (see [Supported Models](index.html#supported-models))
  `messages`      array     Yes        Array of message objects
  `max_tokens`    integer   No         Maximum tokens to generate (default: 100)
  `temperature`   float     No         Sampling temperature 0-2 (default: 1.0)
  `stop`          array     No         Stop sequences
  `tools`         array     No         Tool/function definitions
  `tool_choice`   string    No         Tool selection mode: `auto`, `none`, or specific tool

#### Message Object [​](api-reference.html#message-object){.header-anchor} {#message-object tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-json .vp-adaptive-theme}
[json]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
{
  "role": "user",
  "content": "Hello!"
}
```
:::

  Field            Type     Description
  ---------------- -------- --------------------------------------------------------
  `role`           string   `system`, `user`, `assistant`, or `tool`
  `content`        string   Message content
  `name`           string   Optional name for the participant
  `tool_calls`     array    Tool calls made by assistant (for assistant messages)
  `tool_call_id`   string   ID of tool call being responded to (for tool messages)

### Text Completions [​](api-reference.html#text-completions){.header-anchor} {#text-completions tabindex="-1"}

Generate completions from a text prompt.

::: {.language-http .vp-adaptive-theme}
[http]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
POST /v1/completions
```
:::

#### Request Body [​](api-reference.html#request-body-1){.header-anchor} {#request-body-1 tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-json .vp-adaptive-theme}
[json]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
{
  "model": "openai/gpt-4o",
  "prompt": "Translate to French: Hello, how are you?",
  "max_tokens": 100,
  "temperature": 0.7,
  "stop": ["\n"]
}
```
:::

  Field           Type      Required   Description
  --------------- --------- ---------- ----------------------------
  `model`         string    Yes        Model identifier
  `prompt`        string    Yes        Text prompt
  `max_tokens`    integer   No         Maximum tokens to generate
  `temperature`   float     No         Sampling temperature 0-2
  `stop`          array     No         Stop sequences

Payment Protocol [​](api-reference.html#payment-protocol){.header-anchor} {#payment-protocol tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Step 1: Receive Payment Requirements [​](api-reference.html#step-1-receive-payment-requirements){.header-anchor} {#step-1-receive-payment-requirements tabindex="-1"}

When you make a request without payment, the server responds with
`402 Payment Required`:

::: {.language-http .vp-adaptive-theme}
[http]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
HTTP/1.1 402 Payment Required
Content-Type: application/json
X-PAYMENT-REQUIRED: <base64-encoded-payment-requirements>
```
:::

Decode the `X-PAYMENT-REQUIRED` header to get payment details:

::: {.language-json .vp-adaptive-theme}
[json]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
{
  "scheme": "exact",
  "network": "eip155:84532",
  "maxAmountRequired": "1000000",
  "resource": "https://llm.opengradient.ai/v1/chat/completions",
  "description": "LLM inference payment",
  "mimeType": "application/json",
  "payTo": "0x339c7de83d1a62edafbaac186382ee76584d294f",
  "maxTimeoutSeconds": 300,
  "asset": "0x240b09731D96979f50B2C649C9CE10FcF9C7987F",
  "extra": {
    "name": "OPG",
    "version": "1"
  }
}
```
:::

### Step 2: Create Payment Payload [​](api-reference.html#step-2-create-payment-payload){.header-anchor} {#step-2-create-payment-payload tabindex="-1"}

Create a payment payload matching the EIP-3009
`transferWithAuthorization` format:

::: {.language-json .vp-adaptive-theme}
[json]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
{
  "from": "0xYourWalletAddress",
  "to": "0x339c7de83d1a62edafbaac186382ee76584d294f",
  "value": "1000000",
  "validAfter": 0,
  "validBefore": 1704067200,
  "nonce": "0x1234567890abcdef..."
}
```
:::

### Step 3: Sign the Payment [​](api-reference.html#step-3-sign-the-payment){.header-anchor} {#step-3-sign-the-payment tabindex="-1"}

Sign the payment payload using EIP-712 typed data signing:

**Domain:**

::: {.language-json .vp-adaptive-theme}
[json]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
{
  "name": "OPG",
  "version": "1",
  "chainId": 84532,
  "verifyingContract": "0x240b09731D96979f50B2C649C9CE10FcF9C7987F"
}
```
:::

**Types:**

::: {.language-json .vp-adaptive-theme}
[json]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
{
  "TransferWithAuthorization": [
    {"name": "from", "type": "address"},
    {"name": "to", "type": "address"},
    {"name": "value", "type": "uint256"},
    {"name": "validAfter", "type": "uint256"},
    {"name": "validBefore", "type": "uint256"},
    {"name": "nonce", "type": "bytes32"}
  ]
}
```
:::

### Step 4: Submit Payment [​](api-reference.html#step-4-submit-payment){.header-anchor} {#step-4-submit-payment tabindex="-1"}

Base64-encode the payment object and include it in the `X-PAYMENT`
header:

::: {.language-json .vp-adaptive-theme}
[json]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
{
  "payload": {
    "signature": "0xabcd...",
    "authorization": {
      "from": "0xYourWalletAddress",
      "to": "0x339c7de83d1a62edafbaac186382ee76584d294f",
      "value": "1000000",
      "validAfter": 0,
      "validBefore": 1704067200,
      "nonce": "0x1234567890abcdef..."
    }
  }
}
```
:::

::: {.language-http .vp-adaptive-theme}
[http]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
POST /v1/chat/completions
X-PAYMENT: eyJwYXlsb2FkIjp7...base64-encoded...
Content-Type: application/json

{...request body...}
```
:::

Response Format [​](api-reference.html#response-format){.header-anchor} {#response-format tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Successful Response [​](api-reference.html#successful-response){.header-anchor} {#successful-response tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-http .vp-adaptive-theme}
[http]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: application/json
X-PAYMENT-RESPONSE: <base64-encoded-payment-receipt>
```
:::

#### Chat Completion Response [​](api-reference.html#chat-completion-response){.header-anchor} {#chat-completion-response tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-json .vp-adaptive-theme}
[json]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
{
  "id": "chatcmpl-123",
  "object": "chat.completion",
  "created": 1704067200,
  "model": "openai/gpt-4o",
  "choices": [
    {
      "index": 0,
      "message": {
        "role": "assistant",
        "content": "Hello! How can I help you today?"
      },
      "finish_reason": "stop"
    }
  ],
  "usage": {
    "prompt_tokens": 10,
    "completion_tokens": 8,
    "total_tokens": 18
  }
}
```
:::

#### Text Completion Response [​](api-reference.html#text-completion-response){.header-anchor} {#text-completion-response tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-json .vp-adaptive-theme}
[json]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
{
  "id": "cmpl-123",
  "object": "text_completion",
  "created": 1704067200,
  "model": "openai/gpt-4o",
  "choices": [
    {
      "index": 0,
      "text": "Bonjour, comment allez-vous?",
      "finish_reason": "stop"
    }
  ],
  "usage": {
    "prompt_tokens": 8,
    "completion_tokens": 6,
    "total_tokens": 14
  }
}
```
:::

### Payment Receipt [​](api-reference.html#payment-receipt){.header-anchor} {#payment-receipt tabindex="-1"}

Decode the `X-PAYMENT-RESPONSE` header to get payment settlement
details:

::: {.language-json .vp-adaptive-theme}
[json]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
{
  "success": true,
  "network": "eip155:84532",
  "chainId": 84532,
  "txHash": "0x5678...",
  "payer": "0xYourWalletAddress"
}
```
:::

Error Responses [​](api-reference.html#error-responses){.header-anchor} {#error-responses tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### 402 Payment Required [​](api-reference.html#_402-payment-required){.header-anchor} {#_402-payment-required tabindex="-1"}

Payment needed to proceed. See [Payment
Protocol](api-reference.html#payment-protocol).

### 400 Bad Request [​](api-reference.html#_400-bad-request){.header-anchor} {#_400-bad-request tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-json .vp-adaptive-theme}
[json]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
{
  "error": {
    "message": "Invalid request body",
    "type": "invalid_request_error",
    "code": "invalid_request"
  }
}
```
:::

### 401 Unauthorized [​](api-reference.html#_401-unauthorized){.header-anchor} {#_401-unauthorized tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-json .vp-adaptive-theme}
[json]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
{
  "error": {
    "message": "Invalid payment signature",
    "type": "authentication_error",
    "code": "invalid_signature"
  }
}
```
:::

### 402 Insufficient Funds [​](api-reference.html#_402-insufficient-funds){.header-anchor} {#_402-insufficient-funds tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-json .vp-adaptive-theme}
[json]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
{
  "error": {
    "message": "Insufficient $OPG balance",
    "type": "payment_error",
    "code": "insufficient_funds"
  }
}
```
:::

### 429 Rate Limited [​](api-reference.html#_429-rate-limited){.header-anchor} {#_429-rate-limited tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-json .vp-adaptive-theme}
[json]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
{
  "error": {
    "message": "Rate limit exceeded",
    "type": "rate_limit_error",
    "code": "rate_limited"
  }
}
```
:::

### 500 Internal Error [​](api-reference.html#_500-internal-error){.header-anchor} {#_500-internal-error tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-json .vp-adaptive-theme}
[json]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
{
  "error": {
    "message": "Internal server error",
    "type": "server_error",
    "code": "internal_error"
  }
}
```
:::

Tools / Function Calling [​](api-reference.html#tools-function-calling){.header-anchor} {#tools-function-calling tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Define tools that the model can call:

::: {.language-json .vp-adaptive-theme}
[json]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
{
  "model": "openai/gpt-4o",
  "messages": [
    {"role": "user", "content": "What's the weather in San Francisco?"}
  ],
  "tools": [
    {
      "type": "function",
      "function": {
        "name": "get_weather",
        "description": "Get current weather for a location",
        "parameters": {
          "type": "object",
          "properties": {
            "location": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "City name"
            }
          },
          "required": ["location"]
        }
      }
    }
  ],
  "tool_choice": "auto"
}
```
:::

When the model calls a tool, the response includes:

::: {.language-json .vp-adaptive-theme}
[json]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
{
  "choices": [
    {
      "message": {
        "role": "assistant",
        "content": null,
        "tool_calls": [
          {
            "id": "call_abc123",
            "type": "function",
            "function": {
              "name": "get_weather",
              "arguments": "{\"location\": \"San Francisco\"}"
            }
          }
        ]
      },
      "finish_reason": "tool_calls"
    }
  ]
}
```
:::

Submit tool results by adding a tool message:

::: {.language-json .vp-adaptive-theme}
[json]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
{
  "messages": [
    {"role": "user", "content": "What's the weather in San Francisco?"},
    {
      "role": "assistant",
      "tool_calls": [{"id": "call_abc123", "type": "function", "function": {"name": "get_weather", "arguments": "{\"location\": \"San Francisco\"}"}}]
    },
    {
      "role": "tool",
      "tool_call_id": "call_abc123",
      "content": "{\"temperature\": 65, \"condition\": \"sunny\"}"
    }
  ]
}
```
:::

</div>
:::
:::

::: {.vp-doc ._developers_sdk_ style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

OpenGradient SDK [​](index.html#opengradient-sdk){.header-anchor} {#opengradient-sdk tabindex="-1"}
=================================================================

The OpenGradient SDK enables developers to build applications using
OpenGradient\'s decentralized, end-to-end verified AI infrastructure.
The SDK provides a simple interface to run ML and LLM inference, manage
models, and deploy automated workflows - all secured by the OpenGradient
network.

For LLM inference, the SDK provides a convenient Python wrapper around
the [x402
Gateway](https://docs.opengradient.ai/learn/onchain_inference/llm_execution.html),
handling HTTP requests and payment flows automatically. You can also use
x402 directly from any language if you prefer more control over the
integration. Beyond LLM convenience, the SDK provides additional
capabilities like ML inference, automated workflows, and model
management that are not available through x402 alone.

The SDK is currently available for Python, a TypeScript version is under
development.

::: {.tip .custom-block .github-alert}
TIP

You can check the Python SDK [API
Reference](../../api_reference/python_sdk/index.html) for detailed
methods and types.
:::

In addition to the OpenGradient Python library, the SDK also includes a
CLI tool called `opengradient` for easier access and iteration.

SDK Features [​](index.html#sdk-features){.header-anchor} {#sdk-features tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------

::: {.cards}
[**ML Inference & Workflows**Run ML model inference and deploy automated
workflows that execute on a schedule with live oracle data *(alpha
testnet only)*](ml_inference.html){.card}[**LLM Inference**Secure and
verifiable large language models for completions and chat, with support
for tool usage and TEE execution](llm.html){.card}[**Model
Management**Upload, manage, and organize your models on the Model
Hub](model_management.html){.card}
:::

Installation [​](index.html#installation){.header-anchor} {#installation tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------

To install the OpenGradient Python SDK and CLI, run the following
command:

::: {.language-bash .vp-adaptive-theme}
[bash]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
pip install opengradient
```
:::

You can also find the SDK on
[PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/opengradient/).

::: {.note .custom-block .github-alert}
NOTE

Windows Users: temporarily enable WSL when installing `opengradient`,
fix in progress.
:::

Credentials Setup [​](index.html#credentials-setup){.header-anchor} {#credentials-setup tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------------------

### LLM Inference (Testnet) [​](index.html#llm-inference-testnet){.header-anchor} {#llm-inference-testnet tabindex="-1"}

To run LLM inference, you need an Ethereum-compatible wallet (e.g.,
MetaMask) funded with `$OPG` testnet tokens on **Base Sepolia**.

  Property          Value
  ----------------- -------------------------------------------------------
  Payment Network   Base Sepolia
  Token             `$OPG` (`0x240b09731D96979f50B2C649C9CE10FcF9C7987F`)
  Chain ID          84532

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import opengradient as og

llm = og.LLM(private_key="0x...")
```
:::

::: {.note .custom-block .github-alert}
NOTE

Make sure your wallet has `$OPG` testnet tokens on Base Sepolia. You can
get tokens from our [faucet](https://faucet.opengradient.ai).
:::

### ML Inference (Alpha Testnet) [​](index.html#ml-inference-alpha-testnet){.header-anchor} {#ml-inference-alpha-testnet tabindex="-1"}

To use ML inference and workflows on the alpha testnet, you need a
blockchain account on the OpenGradient alpha testnet. You can use any
existing Ethereum-compatible wallet (e.g., MetaMask) or create a new one
using our SDK (see below).

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import opengradient as og

alpha = og.Alpha(private_key="<private_key>")
```
:::

We provide an account creation wizard in our SDK that guides you through
this process. You can access it by running:

::: {.language-bash .vp-adaptive-theme}
[bash]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
opengradient config init
```
:::

::: {.note .custom-block .github-alert}
NOTE

The wizard will also take you to our [Test
Faucet](https://faucet.opengradient.ai/), where you can fund your
account with testnet tokens.
:::

### Model Hub [​](index.html#model-hub){.header-anchor} {#model-hub tabindex="-1"}

To use model management features, you need a Model Hub account. You can
create one using your email and password on the [Hub Sign
Up](https://hub.opengradient.ai/signup).

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import opengradient as og

hub = og.ModelHub(email="<email>", password="<password>")
```
:::

CLI Setup [​](index.html#cli-setup){.header-anchor} {#cli-setup tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------

Check that the OpenGradient client reflects your settings (private key,
email, etc.)

::: {.language-bash .vp-adaptive-theme}
[bash]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
opengradient config show
```
:::

::: {.note .custom-block .github-alert}
NOTE

You can run `opengradient config init` if your configuration is not
currently set up.
:::

To ensure everything is correctly set up, run the following inference:

::: {.language-bash .vp-adaptive-theme}
[bash]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
opengradient infer -m QmbUqS93oc4JTLMHwpVxsE39mhNxy6hpf6Py3r9oANr8aZ --input '{"num_input1":[1.0, 2.0, 3.0], "num_input2":10, "str_input1":["hello", "ONNX"], "str_input2":" world"}'
```
:::

### More Information [​](index.html#more-information){.header-anchor} {#more-information tabindex="-1"}

To see all commands and parameters we support in the CLI, you can run:

::: {.language-bash .vp-adaptive-theme}
[bash]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
opengradient --help
```
:::

Claude Code Plugin [​](index.html#claude-code-plugin){.header-anchor} {#claude-code-plugin tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------------------

The OpenGradient SDK is also available as a [Claude
Code](https://claude.ai/code) plugin, letting you build with the SDK
directly from your terminal using natural language.

### Install the plugin [​](index.html#install-the-plugin){.header-anchor} {#install-the-plugin tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-bash .vp-adaptive-theme}
[bash]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
claude plugin marketplace add https://github.com/OpenGradient/claude-plugins
```
:::

This registers the OpenGradient marketplace and installs the plugin,
making the `/opengradient-sdk` skill available in your Claude Code
sessions.

### Usage [​](index.html#usage){.header-anchor} {#usage tabindex="-1"}

Invoke the skill directly:

::: {.language- .vp-adaptive-theme}
[]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
/opengradient-sdk write a streaming chat completion with tool calling
```
:::

Or just describe what you want to build with OpenGradient --- Claude
will automatically use the skill when relevant.

::: {.note .custom-block .github-alert}
NOTE

Requires Claude Code installed and a Base Sepolia wallet funded with
`$OPG` tokens for LLM inference.
:::

Code Examples [​](index.html#code-examples){.header-anchor} {#code-examples tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------

You can find examples using the SDK
[here](https://github.com/OpenGradient/OpenGradient-SDK/tree/main/examples).

</div>
:::
:::

<div>

Verifiable LLM Inference via x402 [​](llm.html#verifiable-llm-inference-via-x402){.header-anchor} {#verifiable-llm-inference-via-x402 tabindex="-1"}
=================================================================================================

Overview [​](llm.html#overview){.header-anchor} {#overview tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------

The OpenGradient Python SDK provides a simple interface for running LLM
inference on OpenGradient\'s decentralized network. Under the hood, the
SDK uses x402, a payment-gated HTTP standard that extends HTTP with
payment requirements using the `402 Payment Required` status code.
Inference is paid for using `$OPG` testnet tokens on Base Sepolia, while
execution and verification is handled by the OpenGradient network.

The SDK handles all the complexity of payment signing, verification, and
settlement automatically, so you can focus on building your application.
All LLM inferences are executed on OpenGradient\'s decentralized network
and verified using TEE (Trusted Execution Environments), providing
cryptographic guarantees that your prompts and results are handled
correctly.

Methods [​](llm.html#methods){.header-anchor} {#methods tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------

The SDK currently supports two types of LLM inference:

1.  `llm.completion()` for simple LLM completions (async)
2.  `llm.chat()` for more advanced LLM chat completions including
    tool-usage (async)

Additionally, `llm.ensure_opg_approval()` is used to ensure your wallet
has approved sufficient `$OPG` tokens for Permit2 spending before making
requests (see [Permit2 Approval](llm.html#permit2-approval)).

All LLM inference runs in TEE (Trusted Execution Environment), providing
cryptographic verification of prompts for mission-critical applications
(DeFi, financial services, healthcare, etc.) and ensuring privacy of
personal data through hardware-attested code auditing.

Both of these functions mostly mirror the OpenAI APIs, however there are
some minor differences.

::: {.tip .custom-block .github-alert}
TIP

To learn more about how LLM execution works on OpenGradient, including
the x402 payment-gated API and TEE verification, see [LLM
Execution](https://docs.opengradient.ai/learn/onchain_inference/llm_execution.html).
:::

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
async def completion(
  model: og.TEE_LLM,
  prompt: str,
  max_tokens: int = 100,
  temperature: float = 0.0,
  stop_sequence: Optional[List[str]] = None,
  x402_settlement_mode: og.x402SettlementMode = og.x402SettlementMode.BATCH_HASHED
) -> og.TextGenerationOutput

async def chat(
  model: og.TEE_LLM,
  messages: List[Dict],
  max_tokens: int = 100,
  temperature: float = 0.0,
  stop_sequence: Optional[List[str]] = None,
  tools: Optional[List[Dict]] = None,
  tool_choice: Optional[str] = None,
  x402_settlement_mode: og.x402SettlementMode = og.x402SettlementMode.BATCH_HASHED,
  stream: bool = False
) -> Union[og.TextGenerationOutput, AsyncGenerator[og.StreamChunk, None]]
```
:::

x402 Payment [​](llm.html#x402-payment){.header-anchor} {#x402-payment tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------

LLM inference is paid for using `$OPG` testnet tokens on **Base
Sepolia**. All other operations --- TEE node registration, inference
execution, proof settlement, and verification --- happen on the
**OpenGradient network**. The SDK handles all payment signing and
settlement automatically.

  Property           Value
  ------------------ -------------------------------------------------------
  Payment Network    Base Sepolia
  Token              `$OPG` (`0x240b09731D96979f50B2C649C9CE10FcF9C7987F`)
  Chain ID           84532
  Proof Settlement   OpenGradient Network

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
llm = og.LLM(private_key="0x...")
```
:::

::: {.note .custom-block .github-alert}
NOTE

Make sure your wallet has `$OPG` testnet tokens on Base Sepolia. You can
get tokens from our [faucet](https://faucet.opengradient.ai).
:::

### Permit2 Approval [​](llm.html#permit2-approval){.header-anchor} {#permit2-approval tabindex="-1"}

Before making LLM requests, your wallet must have approved sufficient
`$OPG` tokens for [Permit2](https://github.com/Uniswap/permit2)
spending. Call `llm.ensure_opg_approval()` to set the allowance --- it
only sends an on-chain transaction when the current allowance is below
the requested amount.

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
# Approve at least 5 OPG for Permit2 spending (only transacts if needed)
approval = llm.ensure_opg_approval(opg_amount=5.0)

print(f"Allowance before: {approval.allowance_before}")
print(f"Allowance after: {approval.allowance_after}")
print(f"Tx hash: {approval.tx_hash}")  # None if no approval was needed
```
:::

::: {.tip .custom-block .github-alert}
TIP

You only need to call `ensure_opg_approval` once (or when your allowance
runs low). If the current Permit2 allowance is already sufficient, it
returns immediately without sending a transaction.
:::

Completion Example [​](llm.html#completion-example){.header-anchor} {#completion-example tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------------------

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import asyncio
import opengradient as og
import os

llm = og.LLM(
    private_key=os.environ.get("OG_PRIVATE_KEY"),
)

# Ensure Permit2 allowance for OPG payments
llm.ensure_opg_approval(opg_amount=5.0)

result = asyncio.run(llm.completion(
    model=og.TEE_LLM.GPT_5,
    prompt="Translate the following English text to French: 'Hello, how are you?'",
    max_tokens=50,
    temperature=0.0
))

print(f"Response: {result.completion_output}")
print(f"Payment hash: {result.payment_hash}")
```
:::

Chat Example [​](llm.html#chat-example){.header-anchor} {#chat-example tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import asyncio
import opengradient as og
import os

llm = og.LLM(
    private_key=os.environ.get("OG_PRIVATE_KEY"),
)

# Ensure Permit2 allowance for OPG payments
llm.ensure_opg_approval(opg_amount=5.0)

messages = [
    {
        "role": "system",
        "content": "You are a helpful AI assistant.",
        "name": "HAL"
    },
    {
        "role": "user",
        "content": "Hello! How are you doing? Can you repeat my name?",
    }
]

result = asyncio.run(llm.chat(
    model=og.TEE_LLM.GPT_4_1_2025_04_14,
    messages=messages
))

print(f"Response: {result.chat_output['content']}")
print(f"Payment hash: {result.payment_hash}")
```
:::

Streaming Chat Example [​](llm.html#streaming-chat-example){.header-anchor} {#streaming-chat-example tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import asyncio
import opengradient as og
import os

llm = og.LLM(
    private_key=os.environ.get("OG_PRIVATE_KEY"),
)

llm.ensure_opg_approval(opg_amount=5.0)

async def stream_example():
    messages = [
        {"role": "user", "content": "What is Python?"},
        {"role": "assistant", "content": "Python is a high-level programming language."},
        {"role": "user", "content": "What makes it good for beginners?"},
    ]

    stream = await llm.chat(
        model=og.TEE_LLM.GPT_4_1_2025_04_14,
        messages=messages,
        stream=True,
        max_tokens=300,
    )

    async for chunk in stream:
        if chunk.choices[0].delta.content:
            print(chunk.choices[0].delta.content, end="", flush=True)

asyncio.run(stream_example())
```
:::

Chat Example with Tools [​](llm.html#chat-example-with-tools){.header-anchor} {#chat-example-with-tools tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import asyncio
import opengradient as og
import os

llm = og.LLM(
    private_key=os.environ.get("OG_PRIVATE_KEY"),
)

# Ensure Permit2 allowance for OPG payments
llm.ensure_opg_approval(opg_amount=5.0)

# Define your tools
tools = [{
        "type": "function",
        "function": {
            "name": "get_current_weather",
            "description": "Get the current weather in a given location",
            "parameters": {
                "type": "object",
                "properties": {
                    "city": {
                        "type": "string",
                        "description": "The city to find the weather for, e.g. 'San Francisco'"
                    },
                    "state": {
                        "type": "string",
                        "description": "the two-letter abbreviation for the state that the city is in, e.g. 'CA' which would mean 'California'"
                    },
                    "unit": {
                        "type": "string",
                        "description": "The unit to fetch the temperature in",
                        "enum": ["celsius", "fahrenheit"]
                    }
                },
                "required": ["city", "state", "unit"]
            },
        }
}]

# Message conversation
messages = [
    {
        "role": "system",
        "content": "You are a AI assistant that helps the user with tasks. Use tools if necessary.",
    },
    {
        "role": "user",
        "content": "Hi! How are you doing today?"
    },
    {
        "role": "assistant",
        "content": "I'm doing well! How can I help you?",
    },
    {
        "role": "user",
        "content": "Can you tell me what the temperate will be in Dallas, in fahrenheit?"
    }
]

result = asyncio.run(llm.chat(
    model=og.TEE_LLM.GPT_5,
    messages=messages,
    tools=tools
))

print(f"Response: {result.chat_output['content']}")
print(f"Payment hash: {result.payment_hash}")
```
:::

TEE Verification [​](llm.html#tee-verification){.header-anchor} {#tee-verification tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------------

All LLM inference on OpenGradient runs within trusted execution
environments (TEEs), enabling verified and private access to both
proprietary and open-source models. TEE nodes route requests to
third-party LLM APIs (such as OpenAI, Gemini, Anthropic, and others)
while providing critical security and verification guarantees.

### Key Benefits [​](llm.html#key-benefits){.header-anchor} {#key-benefits tabindex="-1"}

**Verification for Mission-Critical Applications**: TEE nodes enable
prompt verification, making them ideal for mission-critical applications
like DeFi protocols, financial services, healthcare systems, and other
sensitive systems where you need cryptographic proof of what prompts
were sent to the LLM.

**Privacy Protection**: Personal data and sensitive information remain
private. TEE nodes audit and verify code execution, ensuring that your
data is processed securely without exposure to unauthorized parties.

**Hardware Attestation**: Built on Intel TDX with confidential compute,
TEE nodes provide hardware-level attestation of code execution, giving
you cryptographic guarantees that the routing and verification code runs
as expected before forwarding requests to third-party LLM APIs.

::: {.tip .custom-block .github-alert}
TIP

You can verify inference transactions on the [OpenGradient block
explorer](https://explorer.opengradient.ai).
:::

### Examples [​](llm.html#examples){.header-anchor} {#examples tabindex="-1"}

**DeFi Smart Contract Analysis**

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import asyncio
import opengradient as og
import os

llm = og.LLM(
    private_key=os.environ.get("OG_PRIVATE_KEY"),
)

# Ensure Permit2 allowance for OPG payments
llm.ensure_opg_approval(opg_amount=5.0)

# Analyze a smart contract with verified prompts for audit trail
contract_code = """
function transfer(address to, uint256 amount) public {
    require(balanceOf[msg.sender] >= amount, "Insufficient balance");
    balanceOf[msg.sender] -= amount;
    balanceOf[to] += amount;
}
"""

result = asyncio.run(llm.completion(
    model=og.TEE_LLM.GPT_5,
    prompt=f"Analyze this Solidity smart contract function for security vulnerabilities:\n\n{contract_code}",
    max_tokens=500,
    temperature=0.0
))

print(f"Response: {result.completion_output}")
print(f"Payment hash: {result.payment_hash}")
```
:::

**Privacy-Sensitive Healthcare Chat**

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import asyncio
import opengradient as og
import os

llm = og.LLM(
    private_key=os.environ.get("OG_PRIVATE_KEY"),
)

# Ensure Permit2 allowance for OPG payments
llm.ensure_opg_approval(opg_amount=5.0)

# Chat with patient data - TEE ensures privacy and code verification
messages = [
    {
        "role": "system",
        "content": "You are a medical assistant. Analyze patient symptoms and provide preliminary guidance."
    },
    {
        "role": "user",
        "content": "Patient: 45-year-old male, presenting with chest pain and shortness of breath. Blood pressure: 140/90. What are the potential causes?"
    }
]

result = asyncio.run(llm.chat(
    model=og.TEE_LLM.CLAUDE_SONNET_4_6,
    messages=messages,
    max_tokens=300,
    temperature=0.1
))

print(f"Response: {result.chat_output['content']}")
print(f"Payment hash: {result.payment_hash}")
```
:::

**Financial Risk Assessment**

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import asyncio
import opengradient as og
import os

llm = og.LLM(
    private_key=os.environ.get("OG_PRIVATE_KEY"),
)

# Ensure Permit2 allowance for OPG payments
llm.ensure_opg_approval(opg_amount=5.0)

# Assess loan application with verified audit trail
loan_data = {
    "applicant_income": 75000,
    "credit_score": 720,
    "debt_to_income": 0.35,
    "loan_amount": 250000
}

prompt = f"""Assess this loan application for approval:
Income: ${loan_data['applicant_income']}
Credit Score: {loan_data['credit_score']}
Debt-to-Income Ratio: {loan_data['debt_to_income']}
Requested Loan: ${loan_data['loan_amount']}

Provide risk assessment and recommendation."""

result = asyncio.run(llm.completion(
    model=og.TEE_LLM.GPT_5,
    prompt=prompt,
    max_tokens=250,
    temperature=0.0
))

print(f"Response: {result.completion_output}")
print(f"Payment hash: {result.payment_hash}")
```
:::

The TEE node routes your request to the third-party API while providing
cryptographic verification of the prompt and ensuring your data remains
private through hardware-attested code execution.

### Supported Models [​](llm.html#supported-models){.header-anchor} {#supported-models tabindex="-1"}

TEE LLMs support the following models:

-   `openai/gpt-5`
-   `openai/gpt-5-2`
-   `openai/gpt-5-mini`
-   `openai/gpt-4.1-2025-04-14`
-   `openai/o4-mini`
-   `anthropic/claude-opus-4-6`
-   `anthropic/claude-opus-4-5`
-   `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`
-   `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5`
-   `anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5`
-   `google/gemini-3-pro`
-   `google/gemini-3-flash`
-   `google/gemini-2.5-pro`
-   `google/gemini-2.5-flash`
-   `google/gemini-2.5-flash-lite`
-   `x-ai/grok-4`
-   `x-ai/grok-4-fast`
-   `x-ai/grok-4.1-fast`
-   `x-ai/grok-4-1-fast-non-reasoning`

::: {.note .custom-block .github-alert}
NOTE

This technology is cutting-edge, so access may be periodically
restricted due to usage limitations.
:::

Settlement Modes [​](llm.html#settlement-modes){.header-anchor} {#settlement-modes tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------------

The SDK provides three settlement modes via `og.x402SettlementMode` that
control how inference data is recorded on the OpenGradient blockchain
for proof settlement and auditability. Each mode offers different
trade-offs between data completeness, privacy, and transaction costs.

### Available Modes [​](llm.html#available-modes){.header-anchor} {#available-modes tabindex="-1"}

  Mode                Description                                                                                           Use Case
  ------------------- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ---------------------------------------------------------------
  `PRIVATE`           No input or output hashes are posted to the chain. Your inference data remains completely off-chain   Privacy-focused applications where no on-chain data is needed
  `INDIVIDUAL_FULL`   Records full model info, complete input/output data, and all metadata                                 Maximum transparency and auditability
  `BATCH_HASHED`      Aggregates multiple inferences into a single settlement using a Merkle tree                           High-volume applications optimizing for cost

### PRIVATE [​](llm.html#private){.header-anchor} {#private tabindex="-1"}

Individual settlement with no on-chain data. This is the most
privacy-preserving option --- no input or output hashes are posted to
the chain, and your inference data remains completely off-chain.

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
result = await llm.chat(
    model=og.TEE_LLM.GPT_4_1_2025_04_14,
    messages=messages,
    x402_settlement_mode=og.x402SettlementMode.PRIVATE
)
```
:::

### INDIVIDUAL\_FULL [​](llm.html#individual-full){.header-anchor} {#individual-full tabindex="-1"}

Individual settlement with full metadata. Records complete model
information, full input and output data, and all inference metadata
on-chain. Provides maximum transparency and auditability but has higher
gas costs due to larger data storage.

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
result = await llm.chat(
    model=og.TEE_LLM.GPT_4_1_2025_04_14,
    messages=messages,
    x402_settlement_mode=og.x402SettlementMode.INDIVIDUAL_FULL
)
```
:::

Best for:

-   **Proof of Agent Actions**: Cryptographically prove that a specific
    agent used prompt X to take action Y
-   **Resolution Verification**: Verify that decisions used the correct
    prompt with accurate data inputs
-   **Agent Auditing**: Applications requiring complete transparency,
    such as DeFi agents making trading decisions

### BATCH\_HASHED (Default) [​](llm.html#batch-hashed-default){.header-anchor} {#batch-hashed-default tabindex="-1"}

Batch settlement for multiple inferences. Aggregates multiple inference
requests into a single settlement transaction using a Merkle tree with
input/output hashes and signatures. Most cost-efficient for high-volume
applications with reduced per-inference transaction overhead.

::: {.language-python .vp-adaptive-theme}
[python]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
result = await llm.chat(
    model=og.TEE_LLM.GPT_4_1_2025_04_14,
    messages=messages,
    x402_settlement_mode=og.x402SettlementMode.BATCH_HASHED
)
```
:::

::: {.tip .custom-block .github-alert}
TIP

To learn more about how settlement works in the x402 payment protocol,
see [LLM Execution - Settlement
Modes](https://docs.opengradient.ai/learn/onchain_inference/llm_execution.html#settlement-modes).
:::

SDK API Reference [​](llm.html#sdk-api-reference){.header-anchor} {#sdk-api-reference tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------------

Please refer to our [API
Reference](../../api_reference/python_sdk/index.html) for any additional
details around the SDK methods.

More Examples [​](llm.html#more-examples){.header-anchor} {#more-examples tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------

You can find more examples using the SDK
[here](https://github.com/OpenGradient/OpenGradient-SDK/tree/main/examples).

</div>
