::: {.content v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.content-container v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._learn_network_consensus style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

Consensus [​](consensus.html#consensus){.header-anchor} {#consensus tabindex="-1"}
=======================================================

OpenGradient\'s blockchain layer serves as the **verification and
settlement layer** for AI inference and applications - it is not
intended as a general-purpose smart contract platform. The network uses
**CometBFT** consensus with **Cosmos SDK** and **EVM compatibility**,
chosen specifically because they enable the [HACA
architecture\'s](../architecture/index.html) separation of fast
inference execution from asynchronous proof verification.

The blockchain handles amongst other things:

-   **Node Registration**: Verifying and authorizing [inference
    nodes](https://docs.opengradient.ai/learn/architecture/inference_nodes.html)
    and [data
    nodes](https://docs.opengradient.ai/learn/architecture/data_nodes.html)
-   **Proof Settlement**: Validating TEE attestations and ZKML proofs
    after inference completes
-   **Payment Processing**: Settling payments for inference operations
    (for x402 LLM inference, payment settlement happens on Base Sepolia)
-   **Ledger Management**: Maintaining an auditable record of all
    verified AI operations

::: {.note .custom-block .github-alert}
NOTE

In the future, OpenGradient will support smart contracts that can call
AI inference natively via precompiles. This capability is not yet rolled
out on the network.
:::

This design keeps AI inference fast (requests go directly to inference
nodes, not through the blockchain) while ensuring all results are
cryptographically verified and recorded.

Consensus Engine: CometBFT [​](consensus.html#consensus-engine-cometbft){.header-anchor} {#consensus-engine-cometbft tabindex="-1"}
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[CometBFT](https://docs.cometbft.com/v0.38/) is the consensus engine
underlying OpenGradient\'s verification layer. It provides instant
finality and Byzantine fault tolerance - properties that are essential
for AI settlement where results need to be definitively recorded and
verified.

### Key Properties [​](consensus.html#key-properties){.header-anchor} {#key-properties tabindex="-1"}

-   **Instant Finality**: Blocks are final once committed. Inference
    results and proofs are immediately available once settled - no
    confirmation waiting.
-   **Byzantine Fault Tolerance**: The network remains secure as long as
    less than 1/3 of validators are malicious or faulty.
-   **Deterministic Execution**: All validators verify the same proofs
    in the same order, ensuring consistent verification state.
-   **Efficient Verification**: Validators verify proofs and
    attestations without re-executing expensive AI computations.

### How Consensus Works [​](consensus.html#how-consensus-works){.header-anchor} {#how-consensus-works tabindex="-1"}

1.  **Propose**: A validator is selected as the block proposer based on
    their stake weight
2.  **Prevote**: Validators broadcast prevotes for the proposed block
    (including any inference proofs)
3.  **Precommit**: Once 2/3+ prevotes are received, validators broadcast
    precommits
4.  **Commit**: Once 2/3+ precommits are received, the block is
    finalized and proofs are permanently recorded

::: {.mermaid}
:::

Application Layer: Cosmos SDK + EVM [​](consensus.html#application-layer-cosmos-sdk-evm){.header-anchor} {#application-layer-cosmos-sdk-evm tabindex="-1"}
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

OpenGradient uses Cosmos SDK with EVM compatibility - not to compete
with general-purpose blockchains, but to leverage proven infrastructure
and developer tooling for AI-specific functionality.

### Why Cosmos SDK [​](consensus.html#why-cosmos-sdk){.header-anchor} {#why-cosmos-sdk tabindex="-1"}

-   **Modular Architecture**: Build AI-native modules (inference
    verification, node registry, proof settlement) on top of
    battle-tested infrastructure
-   **Flexible State Machine**: Custom application logic for AI
    operations while using proven consensus
-   **Interoperability**: IBC (Inter-Blockchain Communication) enables
    cross-chain AI inference in the future

### Why EVM Compatibility [​](consensus.html#why-evm-compatibility){.header-anchor} {#why-evm-compatibility tabindex="-1"}

EVM compatibility lets developers integrate AI inference into existing
workflows:

-   **Familiar Tooling**: Use Hardhat, Foundry, ethers.js, and MetaMask
    without learning new tools
-   **Smart Contract Integration**: Write AI-enabled smart contracts
    that call inference natively via precompiles (coming soon)
-   **Ecosystem Access**: Bridge assets and interact with existing DeFi
    protocols

Consensus and Inference Verification [​](consensus.html#consensus-and-inference-verification){.header-anchor} {#consensus-and-inference-verification tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The [HACA architecture](../architecture/index.html) separates the **fast
path** (inference execution) from the **verification path**
(settlement). This is fundamental to how OpenGradient achieves web2-like
latency while maintaining decentralized verification:

-   **Inference requests go directly to inference nodes** - not through
    the blockchain
-   **Results are returned immediately** to users with minimal latency
-   **Proofs and attestations are settled asynchronously** on the
    blockchain after inference completes

The blockchain never bottlenecks AI inference. It serves purely as the
verification and settlement layer.

### Settlement Flow [​](consensus.html#settlement-flow){.header-anchor} {#settlement-flow tabindex="-1"}

After an inference node executes a request and generates a proof:

1.  **Inference Completes**: [Inference
    node](https://docs.opengradient.ai/learn/architecture/inference_nodes.html)
    returns results to the user immediately
2.  **Proof Submission**: The inference proof or TEE attestation is
    submitted to the network
3.  **Payment Settlement**: Payment is settled either on the
    OpenGradient chain or on third-party chains like Base
4.  **Block Proposal**: A validator includes the proof in a block
    proposal
5.  **Consensus Verification**: Validators verify the proof during the
    consensus round
6.  **Finalization**: Once 2/3+ validators agree, the proof is
    permanently recorded

::: {.mermaid}
:::

### Verification Methods [​](consensus.html#verification-methods){.header-anchor} {#verification-methods tabindex="-1"}

[Full
nodes](https://docs.opengradient.ai/learn/architecture/full_nodes.html)
verify proofs from inference nodes using different methods depending on
the inference type:

  Method        What Validators Verify                                   Use Case
  ------------- -------------------------------------------------------- -----------------------------------------
  **TEE**       Hardware attestations proving secure enclave execution   LLM inference via third-party providers
  **ZKML**      Zero-knowledge proofs of correct model execution         Local ML model inference
  **Vanilla**   Signature verification only                              Lower-security use cases

Validators verify proofs efficiently without re-executing the actual
inference computation. This keeps consensus fast while ensuring all AI
operations are cryptographically verified.

Inference Node Registry [​](consensus.html#inference-node-registry){.header-anchor} {#inference-node-registry tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Full
nodes](https://docs.opengradient.ai/learn/architecture/full_nodes.html)
maintain an on-chain registry of authorized inference nodes. Before an
inference node can serve requests, it must register with the
network---and for TEE nodes, this registration includes verifying their
hardware attestation.

Registration and attestation verification are handled through smart
contracts, making the entire process auditable and interoperable. This
modular design allows developers to build more complex verification and
orchestration logic on top of the base registry.

### TEE Node Registration [​](consensus.html#tee-node-registration){.header-anchor} {#tee-node-registration tabindex="-1"}

[TEE-enabled inference
nodes](https://docs.opengradient.ai/learn/architecture/inference_nodes.html#1-llm-proxy-nodes)
must prove they\'re running in a secure enclave before they can serve
requests:

1.  **Attestation Generation**: The TEE node generates a hardware
    attestation proving it\'s running correct, untampered software
    inside a secure enclave
2.  **Public Key Publication**: The node publishes its public key along
    with the attestation
3.  **Registry Update**: Full nodes verify the attestation and add the
    node to the registry
4.  **Inference Authorization**: The node can now sign inference results
    that full nodes will accept

::: {.tip .custom-block .github-alert}
TIP

For a detailed breakdown of the TEE Registry smart contract,
verification checks, and secure connection establishment, see [TEE
Verification & Trust
Model](https://docs.opengradient.ai/learn/onchain_inference/llm_execution.html#tee-verification-trust-model).
:::

::: {.mermaid}
:::

### Why Registration Matters [​](consensus.html#why-registration-matters){.header-anchor} {#why-registration-matters tabindex="-1"}

Registration provides the foundation for trust in OpenGradient\'s
inference network:

-   **Privacy Guarantee**: TEE attestation proves the node operator
    cannot log or manipulate user requests
-   **Result Authenticity**: Full nodes only accept results signed by
    registered nodes
-   **Horizontal Scaling**: New inference nodes can join the network by
    completing registration
-   **Revocation**: Compromised nodes can be removed from the registry

Security Model [​](consensus.html#security-model){.header-anchor} {#security-model tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------------

OpenGradient\'s security model is designed around verifiable
AI---ensuring that inference results can be trusted without trusting
individual node operators.

### Inference Verification Security [​](consensus.html#inference-verification-security){.header-anchor} {#inference-verification-security tabindex="-1"}

The primary security guarantee is that AI inference results are
authentic and untampered:

-   **TEE Attestations**: Hardware-backed proof that inference ran in a
    secure enclave where operators cannot see or modify requests
-   **ZKML Proofs**: Mathematical proof that a specific model produced a
    specific output for a given input
-   **Signature Verification**: All inference results are signed by
    registered nodes and verified by full nodes

### Consensus Security [​](consensus.html#consensus-security){.header-anchor} {#consensus-security tabindex="-1"}

The underlying blockchain provides standard BFT security guarantees:

-   **Byzantine Fault Tolerance**: Network remains secure with up to 1/3
    malicious validators
-   **Proof of Stake**: Validators stake tokens; malicious behavior
    results in slashing
-   **Instant Finality**: Once a proof is settled, it cannot be reversed
    or tampered with

### Full Node Responsibilities [​](consensus.html#full-node-responsibilities){.header-anchor} {#full-node-responsibilities tabindex="-1"}

[Full
nodes](https://docs.opengradient.ai/learn/architecture/full_nodes.html)
(validators) have AI-specific responsibilities beyond standard block
validation:

1.  **Verify Inference Proofs**: Validate TEE attestations and ZKML
    proofs from inference nodes
2.  **Maintain Node Registry**: Track authorized inference nodes and
    their attestation status
3.  **Settle Payments**: Process payment settlement for inference
    operations
4.  **Propagate Alerts**: Broadcast warnings when invalid proofs are
    detected

These guarantees ensure users can trust inference results without
trusting individual inference node operators.

Network Parameters [​](consensus.html#network-parameters){.header-anchor} {#network-parameters tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

  Parameter             Value        Description
  --------------------- ------------ -----------------------------------------------------------
  Block Time            10 seconds   Target time between blocks for settlement
  Finality              Instant      Proofs are final once committed---no confirmations needed
  Consensus Threshold   2/3+         Required agreement for block finalization
  Fault Tolerance       \<1/3        Maximum Byzantine validators tolerated

::: {.tip .custom-block .github-alert}
TIP

You can explore the OpenGradient network at
[explorer.opengradient.ai](https://explorer.opengradient.ai).
:::

Next Steps [​](consensus.html#next-steps){.header-anchor} {#next-steps tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------

-   Learn about the [HACA Architecture](../architecture/index.html) and
    how node specialization enables scalable AI inference
-   Understand [Full
    Nodes](https://docs.opengradient.ai/learn/architecture/full_nodes.html)
    and their role in verification and settlement
-   Explore [Inference
    Nodes](https://docs.opengradient.ai/learn/architecture/inference_nodes.html)
    and how they generate proofs

</div>
:::

::: {#doc-outline-aria-label .outline-title aria-level="2" role="heading" v-a5bbad30=""}
On this page
:::
:::

::: {.spacer v-3f215769=""}
:::
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.content v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.content-container v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._twins_smart-contracts style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

Smart Contracts (Reference) [​](smart-contracts.html#smart-contracts-reference){.header-anchor} {#smart-contracts-reference tabindex="-1"}
===============================================================================================

The primary on-chain entry point for Twin.fun is the
`DigitalTwinSharesV1` contract.

Traits & Mixins [​](smart-contracts.html#traits-amp-mixins){.header-anchor} {#traits-amp-mixins tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

-   `Ownable`
-   `AccessControl`
-   `ReentrancyGuard`

Storage Layout [​](smart-contracts.html#storage-layout){.header-anchor} {#storage-layout tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

-   `sharesBalance[id][address] → uint256`
-   `sharesSupply[id] → uint256`
-   `digitalTwinIdToOwner[id] → address`
-   `digitalTwinUrl[id] → string`
-   `digitalTwinExists[id] → bool`
-   `totalDigitalTwins → uint256`

Configurable Parameters [​](smart-contracts.html#configurable-parameters){.header-anchor} {#configurable-parameters tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-   `protocolFeeDestination → address`
-   `protocolFeePercent → uint256` (`1e18 = 100%`)
-   `subjectFeePercent → uint256` (`1e18 = 100%`)
-   `minSharesToCreate → uint256`

### Owner-Only Setters [​](smart-contracts.html#owner-only-setters){.header-anchor} {#owner-only-setters tabindex="-1"}

-   `setFeeDestination(address)`
-   `setProtocolFeePercent(uint256)`
-   `setSubjectFeePercent(uint256)`
-   `setMinSharesToCreate(uint256)`

Roles [​](smart-contracts.html#roles){.header-anchor} {#roles tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------

-   `DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE` - deploy-time admin with full control.
-   `CLAIM_OWNERSHIP_ROLE` - can call `claimOwnership(id, newOwner)` to
    pre-map twin owners.

Events [​](smart-contracts.html#events){.header-anchor} {#events tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------

-   `Trade(trader, id, isBuy, shareAmount, ethAmount, protocolEthAmount, subjectEthAmount, supply)`
-   `DigitalTwinUrlSet(id, url)`
-   `DigitalTwinOwnershipClaimed(id, newOwner)`

Read Functions [​](smart-contracts.html#read-functions){.header-anchor} {#read-functions tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

-   `getPrice(uint256 supply, uint256 amount)`
-   `getBuyPrice(bytes16 id, uint256 amount)`
-   `getSellPrice(bytes16 id, uint256 amount)`
-   `getBuyPriceAfterFee(bytes16 id, uint256 amount)`
-   `getSellPriceAfterFee(bytes16 id, uint256 amount)`

Write Functions [​](smart-contracts.html#write-functions){.header-anchor} {#write-functions tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

-   `setDigitalTwinUrl(bytes16 id, string url)` - twin owner only.
-   `createDigitalTwin(bytes16 id, string url)` - payable helper to set
    the URL and seed minimum liquidity.
-   `buyShares(bytes16 id, uint256 amount)` - payable, `nonReentrant`.
-   `sellShares(bytes16 id, uint256 amount)` - `nonReentrant`.

Security Notes [​](smart-contracts.html#security-notes){.header-anchor} {#security-notes tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

-   `nonReentrant` guards on buy and sell.
-   ETH payouts use direct calls to the recipient address.
-   Fee percentages are owner-settable (no on-chain caps); integrate
    with appropriate warnings in UI.

</div>
:::
:::

::: {.vp-doc ._twins_ style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

Twin.fun Overview [​](index.html#twin-fun-overview){.header-anchor} {#twin-fun-overview tabindex="-1"}
===================================================================

::: {.hero}
::: {.hero-content}
Twin.fun - Trade Minds, Not Tokens

Create and trade access to AI "digital twins" via key markets on a transparent bonding curve.
=============================================================================================

::: {.hero-actions}
[Get Started](https://www.twin.fun/){.button .primary}
:::
:::
:::

Overview [​](index.html#overview){.header-anchor} {#overview tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------

Twin.fun is a marketplace for AI-powered **digital twins** - agents
modeled after real people or personas. Each twin has a **key market**
(also called shares), where users buy or sell keys on a deterministic
bonding curve. Holding keys unlocks gated experiences (chat, tools,
utilities) tied to that twin.

### Why Twin.fun [​](index.html#why-twin-fun){.header-anchor} {#why-twin-fun tabindex="-1"}

-   **Market-driven access** - transparent pricing and liquidity on a
    bonding curve.
-   **Aligned incentives** - fees flow to the creator (twin owner) and
    keep the protocol sustainable.
-   **Composable data** - an indexer/subgraph powers analytics, bots,
    and integrations.

### High-Level Architecture [​](index.html#high-level-architecture){.header-anchor} {#high-level-architecture tabindex="-1"}

![Contracts, indexing, and application layers work together to power key
markets.](../assets/high-level.PP10ChTs.png)

Explore the Twin.fun Stack [​](index.html#explore-the-twin-fun-stack){.header-anchor} {#explore-the-twin-fun-stack tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

::: {.cards}
[**Core Concepts**Terminology, primitives, and protocol
guarantees.](key_concepts.html){.card}[**Architecture**Contracts, the
indexer, and the app flow.](architecture.html){.card}[**Lifecycle**From
twin creation to key redemption.](lifecycle.html){.card}[**Participants
& Roles**Responsibilities for creators, traders, developers, and the
protocol.](roles.html){.card}[**Creator Guide**Claim IDs, configure
metadata, and launch gated utilities.](creators.html){.card}[**Trader
Guide**Buy, hold, and sell keys with best
practices.](traders.html){.card}[**Pricing & Fees**Bonding curve math,
fee splits, and creation thresholds.](pricing.html){.card}[**Smart
Contracts**Storage layout, events, and admin
controls.](smart-contracts.html){.card}[**Networks &
Addresses**Deployment references across
environments.](networks.html){.card}[**Developer Quickstart**Read
prices, execute trades, and integrate with the
ABI.](developer-quickstart.html){.card}[**Subgraph & Data**Entities,
queries, and endpoints for
analytics.](subgraph-data.html){.card}[**Building Apps**Integrations,
access control, and live event
strategies.](building-apps.html){.card}[**App Features**Explore, twin
pages, leaderboards, and
portfolios.](app-features.html){.card}[**Security & Risk**Non-custodial
guarantees, limitations, and
disclosures.](security.html){.card}[**Policy & Legal**Disclaimers,
impersonation policies, and acceptable
metadata.](policy.html){.card}[**Roadmap & Changelog**Planned milestones
and release history.](roadmap.html){.card}[**FAQ**Answers to the most
common protocol questions.](faq.html){.card}[**Glossary**Definitions for
Twin.fun terminology.](glossary.html){.card}
:::

Quick Paths [​](index.html#quick-paths){.header-anchor} {#quick-paths tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------

-   **New to Twin.fun?** Start with the [Core
    Concepts](key_concepts.html) and [Lifecycle](lifecycle.html).
-   **Launching a twin?** Dive into the [Creator Guide](creators.html)
    and [Pricing & Fees](pricing.html).
-   **Integrating programmatically?** Jump to the [Developer
    Quickstart](developer-quickstart.html) and [Subgraph &
    Data](subgraph-data.html) references.

</div>
:::

::: {#doc-outline-aria-label .outline-title aria-level="2" role="heading" v-a5bbad30=""}
On this page
:::
:::

::: {.spacer v-3f215769=""}
:::
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.content v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.content-container v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._twins_roles style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

Participant Roles [​](roles.html#participant-roles){.header-anchor} {#participant-roles tabindex="-1"}
===================================================================

Twin.fun aligns incentives across creators, traders, developers, and the
protocol.

Creators / Subjects [​](roles.html#creators-subjects){.header-anchor} {#creators-subjects tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------------------

-   Receive **subject fees** on each trade.
-   Configure metadata URLs and brand assets.
-   Can be pre-mapped as the only address allowed to trigger the first
    buy at zero supply.

Users / Traders [​](roles.html#users-traders){.header-anchor} {#users-traders tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------------

-   Buy and sell keys to access gated utilities.
-   Appear on holder lists, activity feeds, and leaderboards.
-   Monitor curve pricing, fees, and liquidity via the subgraph or
    community dashboards.

Developers [​](roles.html#developers){.header-anchor} {#developers tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------

-   Integrate price feeds, verify key ownership, and stream trades.
-   Build analytics dashboards, bots, and companion utilities.
-   Consume subgraph data or call view functions directly on the
    contract.

Protocol Team [​](roles.html#protocol-team){.header-anchor} {#protocol-team tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------

-   Configures fee percentages, fee destinations, and minimum create
    amounts.
-   Maintains the contract deployments, app backend, and reference
    subgraph.
-   Oversees compliance, security monitoring, and upgrades.

</div>
:::
:::

::: {#doc-outline-aria-label .outline-title aria-level="2" role="heading" v-a5bbad30=""}
On this page
:::
:::

::: {.spacer v-3f215769=""}
:::
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.content v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.content-container v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._twins_app-features style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

App Features (User Guide) [​](app-features.html#app-features-user-guide){.header-anchor} {#app-features-user-guide tabindex="-1"}
========================================================================================

Twin.fun's interface highlights discovery, trading, and portfolio
management for digital twins.

Explore [​](app-features.html#explore){.header-anchor} {#explore tabindex="-1"}
------------------------------------------------------

Browse twins, sort by volume, price change, holders, and other momentum
metrics.

Twin Page [​](app-features.html#twin-page){.header-anchor} {#twin-page tabindex="-1"}
----------------------------------------------------------

View price, supply, top holders, recent trades, and a panel for
chat/utilities gated by key ownership.

Leaderboard [​](app-features.html#leaderboard){.header-anchor} {#leaderboard tabindex="-1"}
--------------------------------------------------------------

Ranks twins by volume or velocity (when available) with refresh cadence
noted in the UI.

Portfolio [​](app-features.html#portfolio){.header-anchor} {#portfolio tabindex="-1"}
----------------------------------------------------------

Shows owned twins, balances, and (if live) P&L approximations to track
performance.

> Features marked *coming soon* are labeled directly in the interface.

</div>
:::
:::

::: {.content v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.content-container v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._twins_subgraph-data style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

Subgraph & Data [​](subgraph-data.html#subgraph-amp-data){.header-anchor} {#subgraph-amp-data tabindex="-1"}
=========================================================================

Twin.fun's subgraph indexes trades, holders, and protocol stats for
analytics and integrations.

Entities [​](subgraph-data.html#entities){.header-anchor} {#entities tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------

-   `DigitalTwin` - id, owner, URL, currentSupply, prices, caps,
    volumes, trades.
-   `Trade` - `isBuy`, `shareAmount`, `ethAmount`, `pricePerShare`,
    `totalCost`, `supplyAfter`, `timestamp`, `trader`.
-   `Trader` - aggregate buys, sells, fees, first/last activity.
-   `Holder` - balance per twin, totals, first/last actions.
-   `Candle` - minute/5m/hour/day OHLC with volumes and trade counts.
-   `ProtocolStats` - protocol-wide totals.

Example Queries [​](subgraph-data.html#example-queries){.header-anchor} {#example-queries tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Get a Twin [​](subgraph-data.html#get-a-twin){.header-anchor} {#get-a-twin tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-graphql .vp-adaptive-theme}
[graphql]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
query GetDigitalTwin($id: ID!) {
  digitalTwin(id: $id) {
    id owner url currentSupply lastPrice marketPrice marketCap
    totalVolumeETH totalTrades totalBuys totalSells createdAt lastTradeAt
    holders(first: 5, orderBy: balance, orderDirection: desc) {
      trader { id }
      balance
    }
  }
}
```
:::

### Recent Trades [​](subgraph-data.html#recent-trades){.header-anchor} {#recent-trades tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-graphql .vp-adaptive-theme}
[graphql]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
query TwinTrades($id: ID!) {
  trades(first: 25, orderBy: timestamp, orderDirection: desc,
         where: { digitalTwin: $id }) {
    isBuy shareAmount ethAmount pricePerShare totalCost supplyAfter timestamp
    trader { id }
  }
}
```
:::

### Top Holders [​](subgraph-data.html#top-holders){.header-anchor} {#top-holders tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-graphql .vp-adaptive-theme}
[graphql]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
query TopHolders {
  holders(first: 20, orderBy: balance, orderDirection: desc) {
    id balance
    digitalTwin { id owner }
    trader { id }
  }
}
```
:::

Endpoints [​](subgraph-data.html#endpoints){.header-anchor} {#endpoints tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------

-   **Base Sepolia:** `<your-subgraph-url>`
-   **Mainnet:** `<your-subgraph-url>`

</div>
:::
:::

::: {#doc-outline-aria-label .outline-title aria-level="2" role="heading" v-a5bbad30=""}
On this page
:::
:::

::: {.spacer v-3f215769=""}
:::
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.content v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.content-container v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._twins_building-apps style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

Building Apps on Twin.fun [​](building-apps.html#building-apps-on-twin-fun){.header-anchor} {#building-apps-on-twin-fun tabindex="-1"}
===========================================================================================

Use the bonding curve contract and subgraph to build dashboards, bots,
and custom experiences.

Read-Only Integrations [​](building-apps.html#read-only-integrations){.header-anchor} {#read-only-integrations tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-   Surface prices, candles, trades, and holders from the subgraph.
-   Display supply, last trade price, estimated slippage, and fees.

Access Control [​](building-apps.html#access-control){.header-anchor} {#access-control tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------------------

-   Verify key ownership by reading `sharesBalance[id][user]` on-chain
    or consuming the `Holder` entity from the subgraph.
-   Gate features based on balance thresholds (commonly ≥1 key).

Event-Driven UIs [​](building-apps.html#event-driven-uis){.header-anchor} {#event-driven-uis tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

-   Subscribe to the `Trade` event to update prices, holders, and
    activity feeds in real time.
-   Combine event streams with subgraph polling for resilient UX.

Security Checklist [​](building-apps.html#security-checklist){.header-anchor} {#security-checklist tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

-   Display fee and value breakdowns before executing buys.
-   Warn users that `buyShares` requires the exact `msg.value`.
-   Handle chain reorgs by waiting for confirmations on critical UX
    state.

</div>
:::
:::

::: {.VPDoc .has-sidebar .has-aside v-1428d186="" v-39a288b8=""}

::: {.VPDocAside v-39a288b8="" v-3f215769=""}

::: {.outline-marker v-a5bbad30=""}
:::

::: {#doc-outline-aria-label .outline-title aria-level="2" role="heading" v-a5bbad30=""}
On this page
:::
:::

::: {.spacer v-3f215769=""}
:::
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.content v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.content-container v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._twins_faq style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

FAQ [​](faq.html#faq){.header-anchor} {#faq tabindex="-1"}
=====================================

Are keys ERC-20 or ERC-721 tokens? [​](faq.html#are-keys-erc-20-or-erc-721-tokens){.header-anchor} {#are-keys-erc-20-or-erc-721-tokens tabindex="-1"}
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

No. Balances are tracked in contract mappings per twin; positions change
only via the buy/sell bonding curve functions.

How do fees work? [​](faq.html#how-do-fees-work){.header-anchor} {#how-do-fees-work tabindex="-1"}
----------------------------------------------------------------

Each trade splits fees between the protocol treasury and the twin owner,
using percentages set by the contract owner.

Can the owner change fees later? [​](faq.html#can-the-owner-change-fees-later){.header-anchor} {#can-the-owner-change-fees-later tabindex="-1"}
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Yes. Updates apply to future trades only.

What happens if I overpay when buying? [​](faq.html#what-happens-if-i-overpay-when-buying){.header-anchor} {#what-happens-if-i-overpay-when-buying tabindex="-1"}
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Excess `msg.value` is not refunded - send the exact amount returned by
the pricing helpers.

Do you support multiple chains? [​](faq.html#do-you-support-multiple-chains){.header-anchor} {#do-you-support-multiple-chains tabindex="-1"}
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Testnet support exists on Base Sepolia; mainnet deployments are to be
announced.

How do I verify my metadata URL? [​](faq.html#how-do-i-verify-my-metadata-url){.header-anchor} {#how-do-i-verify-my-metadata-url tabindex="-1"}
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you are the mapped owner, call `setDigitalTwinUrl(id, url)`; the app
reads the value directly on-chain.

</div>
:::
:::

::: {.vp-doc ._twins_developer-quickstart style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

Developer Quickstart [​](developer-quickstart.html#developer-quickstart){.header-anchor} {#developer-quickstart tabindex="-1"}
========================================================================================

Kick-start integrations with the Twin.fun bonding curve contract using
ethers v6.

Setup [​](developer-quickstart.html#setup){.header-anchor} {#setup tabindex="-1"}
----------------------------------------------------------

-   Install dependencies (ethers v6 recommended).
-   Configure RPC access for Base Sepolia and/or mainnet deployments.
-   Import the ABI for `DigitalTwinSharesV1`.

Read Price & Buy (ethers v6) [​](developer-quickstart.html#read-price-amp-buy-ethers-v6){.header-anchor} {#read-price-amp-buy-ethers-v6 tabindex="-1"}
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

::: {.language-ts .vp-adaptive-theme}
[ts]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
import { ethers } from "ethers";
import abi from "./DigitalTwinSharesV1.abi.json";

const provider = new ethers.JsonRpcProvider(process.env.RPC_URL!);
const wallet   = new ethers.Wallet(process.env.PRIVATE_KEY!, provider);
const addr     = "0x065fb766051c9a212218c9D5e8a9B83fb555C17c"; // Base Sepolia
const c        = new ethers.Contract(addr, abi, wallet);

const id = "0x85f4f72079114bfcac1003134e5424f4"; // bytes16
await (async () => {
  const amount   = 1n;
  const price    = await c.getBuyPrice(id, amount);
  const feeP     = await c.protocolFeePercent();
  const feeS     = await c.subjectFeePercent();
  const protocol = (price * feeP) / (10n ** 18n);
  const subject  = (price * feeS) / (10n ** 18n);
  const value    = price + protocol + subject;
  const tx = await c.buyShares(id, amount, { value });
  await tx.wait();
})();
```
:::

Sell & Estimate Proceeds [​](developer-quickstart.html#sell-amp-estimate-proceeds){.header-anchor} {#sell-amp-estimate-proceeds tabindex="-1"}
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

::: {.language-ts .vp-adaptive-theme}
[ts]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
const amount = 1n;
const after  = await c.getSellPriceAfterFee(id, amount);
const tx     = await c.sellShares(id, amount);
await tx.wait();
```
:::

Common Errors & Fixes [​](developer-quickstart.html#common-errors-amp-fixes){.header-anchor} {#common-errors-amp-fixes tabindex="-1"}
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-   **Insufficient value:** compute `getBuyPriceAfterFee` and send the
    exact `msg.value`.
-   **Creation guard:** at zero supply, `amount` must be ≥
    `minSharesToCreate`.
-   **Owner required:** the twin is pre-claimed; the mapped owner must
    perform the first buy.

</div>
:::
:::

::: {.items v-b7550ba0=""}

:::

::: {.VPSidebarItem .level-1 .is-link v-b7550ba0="" v-b7550ba0=""}
::: {.item v-b7550ba0=""}
::: {.indicator v-b7550ba0=""}

Core Features

::: {.content v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.content-container v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._twins_security style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

Security & Risk [​](security.html#security-amp-risk){.header-anchor} {#security-amp-risk tabindex="-1"}
====================================================================

Twin.fun is non-custodial: ETH settles directly between traders,
creators, and the protocol treasury.

Key Properties [​](security.html#key-properties){.header-anchor} {#key-properties tabindex="-1"}
----------------------------------------------------------------

-   `buyShares` and `sellShares` are `nonReentrant`.
-   Events provide transparent logs for indexing and analytics.
-   No custodial balances - funds flow through the contract in a single
    transaction.

Known Limitations [​](security.html#known-limitations){.header-anchor} {#known-limitations tabindex="-1"}
----------------------------------------------------------------------

-   The contract does not refund overpaid `msg.value`; callers must send
    the exact amount.
-   Fee percentages are owner-settable and can change for future trades.
-   Keys are non-transferable; positions adjust only via buy/sell
    functions.

Mitigations [​](security.html#mitigations){.header-anchor} {#mitigations tabindex="-1"}
----------------------------------------------------------

-   Clear event logs enable monitoring and analytics.
-   Creator ownership can be pre-mapped to prevent impersonation on
    initial buys.

Responsible Disclosure [​](security.html#responsible-disclosure){.header-anchor} {#responsible-disclosure tabindex="-1"}
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-   Security contact: <security@twin.fun> *(placeholder)*.
-   Bug bounty: provide relevant links if/when available.

</div>
:::
:::

::: {#doc-outline-aria-label .outline-title aria-level="2" role="heading" v-a5bbad30=""}
On this page
:::
:::

::: {.spacer v-3f215769=""}
:::
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.content v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.content-container v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._twins_creators style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

Creator Guide [​](creators.html#creator-guide){.header-anchor} {#creator-guide tabindex="-1"}
==============================================================

Claim your identity, configure metadata, and launch experiences for your
community.

What You Get [​](creators.html#what-you-get){.header-anchor} {#what-you-get tabindex="-1"}
------------------------------------------------------------

-   Revenue share on every trade via subject fees.
-   A transparent market for your AI twin with deterministic pricing.
-   Analytics via the subgraph and community dashboards.

Claim Your Twin ID [​](creators.html#claim-your-twin-id){.header-anchor} {#claim-your-twin-id tabindex="-1"}
------------------------------------------------------------------------

-   Each twin is identified by a 16-byte `bytes16` ID.
-   The protocol can pre-map ownership so only the designated address
    can perform the first buy.
-   Without a pre-map, the first buyer at zero supply becomes the owner.

Set Metadata URL [​](creators.html#set-metadata-url){.header-anchor} {#set-metadata-url tabindex="-1"}
--------------------------------------------------------------------

Use `setDigitalTwinUrl(id, url)` to reference a JSON descriptor. Store
metadata on Walrus for decentralized durability.

### Recommended JSON Schema [​](creators.html#recommended-json-schema){.header-anchor} {#recommended-json-schema tabindex="-1"}

::: {.language-json .vp-adaptive-theme}
[json]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
{
  "name": "<display name>",
  "description": "<short description>",
  "avatar": "https://.../image.png",
  "links": {
    "website": "https://...",
    "x": "https://x.com/...",
    "discord": "https://discord.gg/..."
  },
  "tags": ["creator", "ai", "research"]
}
```
:::

Minimum Liquidity to Create [​](creators.html#minimum-liquidity-to-create){.header-anchor} {#minimum-liquidity-to-create tabindex="-1"}
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-   At zero supply, the first buy must be **≥ `minSharesToCreate`**.
-   `createDigitalTwin(id, url)` is a helper that buys the minimum and
    sets metadata in one transaction.

Best Practices [​](creators.html#best-practices){.header-anchor} {#best-practices tabindex="-1"}
----------------------------------------------------------------

-   Use stable Walrus URLs for metadata.
-   Keep preview images lightweight (\< 1 MB).
-   Avoid redirects that may break wallet previews.

Troubleshooting [​](creators.html#troubleshooting){.header-anchor} {#troubleshooting tabindex="-1"}
------------------------------------------------------------------

-   **URL not updating?** Ensure you are the owner and re-submit
    `setDigitalTwinUrl` with the correct ID.
-   **"Owner required" on first buy?** The ID is pre-claimed; the mapped
    owner must make the initial purchase.

</div>
:::
:::

::: {.VPDoc .has-sidebar .has-aside v-1428d186="" v-39a288b8=""}

::: {#doc-outline-aria-label .outline-title aria-level="2" role="heading" v-a5bbad30=""}
On this page
:::
:::

::: {.spacer v-3f215769=""}
:::
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.content v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.content-container v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._twins_key_concepts style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

Core Concepts [​](key_concepts.html#core-concepts){.header-anchor} {#core-concepts tabindex="-1"}
==================================================================

Twin.fun introduces a few critical primitives that power the
marketplace.

Digital Twin [​](key_concepts.html#digital-twin){.header-anchor} {#digital-twin tabindex="-1"}
----------------------------------------------------------------

An AI agent referenced on-chain by a 16-byte **twin ID** (`bytes16`).
Metadata for each twin lives off-chain on Walrus - our decentralized
storage partner - and is surfaced inside the app experience.

Keys [​](key_concepts.html#keys){.header-anchor} {#keys tabindex="-1"}
------------------------------------------------

Deterministic units tracked directly in contract mappings. Users acquire
or redeem keys by calling bonding curve functions. Holding ≥1 key
unlocks gated experiences tied to that twin. Keys were previously
referred to as \"shares\" in early contract versions - you may still see
`buyShares` and `sellShares` in the ABI.

Twin Owner [​](key_concepts.html#twin-owner){.header-anchor} {#twin-owner tabindex="-1"}
------------------------------------------------------------

The address that receives the **subject fee** on each trade. Owners can
update metadata URLs and manage brand assets for their twin.

Protocol Fees [​](key_concepts.html#protocol-fees){.header-anchor} {#protocol-fees tabindex="-1"}
------------------------------------------------------------------

A percentage of every trade is directed to the treasury address. Fee
parameters are configurable by the protocol owner.

Bonding Curve [​](key_concepts.html#bonding-curve){.header-anchor} {#bonding-curve tabindex="-1"}
------------------------------------------------------------------

Twin.fun uses a quadratic, FriendTech-style curve where marginal price
increases with supply. Buying keys pushes supply up the curve; selling
does the opposite.

Gated Utilities [​](key_concepts.html#gated-utilities){.header-anchor} {#gated-utilities tabindex="-1"}
----------------------------------------------------------------------

App features - chat, tools, or custom integrations - unlock when an
address holds keys for a specific twin.

Subgraph / Indexer [​](key_concepts.html#subgraph-indexer){.header-anchor} {#subgraph-indexer tabindex="-1"}
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

The off-chain data layer that streams trades, holders, and analytics to
the app and any external builders.

</div>
:::
:::

::: {#doc-outline-aria-label .outline-title aria-level="2" role="heading" v-a5bbad30=""}
On this page

::: {.spacer v-3f215769=""}

:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.content v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.content-container v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._twins_policy style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

Policy, Legal & Disclaimers [​](policy.html#policy-legal-amp-disclaimers){.header-anchor} {#policy-legal-amp-disclaimers tabindex="-1"}
=========================================================================================

Twin.fun keys provide access utility - they are not financial products.

Access Instrument [​](policy.html#access-instrument){.header-anchor} {#access-instrument tabindex="-1"}
--------------------------------------------------------------------

-   Keys grant entry to gated experiences; they are meant for
    participation, not speculation.

Jurisdictions [​](policy.html#jurisdictions){.header-anchor} {#jurisdictions tabindex="-1"}
------------------------------------------------------------

-   Users are responsible for complying with local laws and regulations.

Impersonation & Abuse [​](policy.html#impersonation-amp-abuse){.header-anchor} {#impersonation-amp-abuse tabindex="-1"}
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-   Pre-claim ownership plus verification flows reduce impersonation
    risk.
-   Report abuse or suspicious activity to <support@twin.fun>
    *(placeholder).*

Metadata Guidelines [​](policy.html#metadata-guidelines){.header-anchor} {#metadata-guidelines tabindex="-1"}
------------------------------------------------------------------------

-   No illegal content, malware, or deceptive links in metadata URLs.
-   Prefer immutable storage on [Walrus](https://www.walrus.xyz/) where
    possible.

</div>
:::
:::

::: {#doc-outline-aria-label .outline-title aria-level="2" role="heading" v-a5bbad30=""}
On this page
:::
:::

::: {.spacer v-3f215769=""}
:::
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.content v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.content-container v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._twins_architecture style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

Architecture [​](architecture.html#architecture){.header-anchor} {#architecture tabindex="-1"}
================================================================

Twin.fun combines on-chain contracts with indexing infrastructure and
the application client.

High-Level Flow [​](architecture.html#high-level-flow){.header-anchor} {#high-level-flow tabindex="-1"}
----------------------------------------------------------------------

![Twin.fun routes trades through the bonding curve contract while
downstream systems deliver real-time context to the
client.](../assets/high-level.PP10ChTs.png)

1.  **Users / Wallets** initiate `buyShares` or `sellShares`
    transactions against the bonding curve.
2.  **DigitalTwinSharesV1** maintains balances, supply, and fee routing
    while emitting events for downstream consumers.
3.  **Subgraph / Indexer** ingests contract events to expose prices,
    holders, trades, candles, and protocol stats.
4.  **App Backend / Client** renders the user experience and verifies
    key ownership for access control.

</div>
:::
:::

::: {.vp-doc ._twins_roadmap style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

Roadmap & Changelog [​](roadmap.html#roadmap-amp-changelog){.header-anchor} {#roadmap-amp-changelog tabindex="-1"}
===========================================================================

Stay up to date with planned milestones and historical releases.

Roadmap (High Level) [​](roadmap.html#roadmap-high-level){.header-anchor} {#roadmap-high-level tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

-   Mainnet deployment(s).
-   Leaderboards & advanced analytics.
-   Expanded candles & API endpoints.
-   Creator tools (claim and verification flows).

Changelog [​](roadmap.html#changelog){.header-anchor} {#changelog tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------

-   **v1.0 (Draft):** Initial Twin.fun documentation covering contracts,
    bonding curve math, subgraph, and developer paths.

</div>
:::
:::

::: {.content v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.content-container v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._twins_lifecycle style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

Lifecycle [​](lifecycle.html#lifecycle){.header-anchor} {#lifecycle tabindex="-1"}
=======================================================

From launch to ongoing trading, each twin follows a predictable flow on
Twin.fun.

Stages [​](lifecycle.html#stages){.header-anchor} {#stages tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------

1.  **Create or Claim a Twin**
    -   Use a `bytes16` twin ID.
    -   Pre-map ownership if applicable and set the metadata URL via
        `createDigitalTwin` or `setDigitalTwinUrl`.
2.  **Buy Keys**
    -   Price is computed from current supply using the quadratic
        bonding curve.
    -   Buying increases supply and price; protocol and subject fees are
        taken on every trade.
3.  **Use Access**
    -   The app checks your key balance for that twin and unlocks chat,
        tools, and other utilities.
4.  **Sell Keys**
    -   Receive ETH based on the lower supply after selling, minus
        protocol and subject fees.

Sequence Diagram [​](lifecycle.html#sequence-diagram){.header-anchor} {#sequence-diagram tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------------------

![The sequence diagram highlights creation, trading, fee distribution,
and key-gated access checks.](../assets/uml.UBZi2cLy.png)

</div>
:::
:::

::: {#doc-outline-aria-label .outline-title aria-level="2" role="heading" v-a5bbad30=""}
On this page
:::
:::

::: {.spacer v-3f215769=""}
:::
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.content v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.content-container v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._twins_glossary style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

Glossary [​](glossary.html#glossary){.header-anchor} {#glossary tabindex="-1"}
====================================================

Definitions for key Twin.fun terminology.

-   **Twin ID:** 16-byte identifier (`bytes16`) that uniquely references
    a digital twin on-chain.
-   **Key (Share):** Unit of access for a twin, priced by the bonding
    curve and recorded in contract mappings.
-   **Subject Fee:** Fee share paid to the twin owner on every trade.
-   **Protocol Fee:** Fee share routed to the protocol treasury on every
    trade.
-   **MinSharesToCreate:** Minimum keys required on the first buy when
    supply is zero.
-   **Bonding Curve:** Quadratic price function where marginal price
    increases with supply.
-   **Subgraph:** Off-chain index of on-chain events used for analytics,
    dashboards, and integrations.

</div>
:::
:::

::: {#doc-outline-aria-label .outline-title aria-level="2" role="heading" v-a5bbad30=""}
On this page
:::
:::

::: {.spacer v-3f215769=""}
:::
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.content v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.content-container v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._twins_traders style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

Trader Guide [​](traders.html#trader-guide){.header-anchor} {#trader-guide tabindex="-1"}
===========================================================

Buy, hold, and sell keys confidently on Twin.fun.

Utility [​](traders.html#utility){.header-anchor} {#utility tabindex="-1"}
-------------------------------------------------

Holding ≥1 key for a twin unlocks that twin's gated features in the app
(chat, tools, utilities).

Buying Keys [​](traders.html#buying-keys){.header-anchor} {#buying-keys tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------

-   Call `getBuyPriceAfterFee(id, amount)` to estimate required ETH.
-   Execute `buyShares(id, amount)` with the exact `msg.value` returned
    by the pricing helper.
-   The contract does **not** refund overpayment.

Selling Keys [​](traders.html#selling-keys){.header-anchor} {#selling-keys tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------

-   Call `getSellPriceAfterFee(id, amount)` before exiting to preview
    proceeds.
-   Use `sellShares(id, amount)` to redeem keys for ETH at the lower,
    post-sale supply.

Reading the Market [​](traders.html#reading-the-market){.header-anchor} {#reading-the-market tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

-   Track supply, last trade price, OHLC candles, top holders, and
    volume through the subgraph.
-   Community dashboards and bots surface analytics powered by the same
    indexed data.

Risks [​](traders.html#risks){.header-anchor} {#risks tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------

-   Bonding curve prices move with order flow; liquidity is
    deterministic but not constant.
-   Keys grant access to experiences - they are not traditional
    financial instruments.

</div>
:::
:::

::: {#VPContent .VPContent .has-sidebar v-5d98c3a5="" v-1428d186=""}
::: {.VPDoc .has-sidebar .has-aside v-1428d186="" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.container v-39a288b8=""}

::: {.aside-container v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.aside-content v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.VPDocAside v-39a288b8="" v-3f215769=""}

::: {.outline-marker v-a5bbad30=""}
:::

::: {#doc-outline-aria-label .outline-title aria-level="2" role="heading" v-a5bbad30=""}
On this page
:::
:::

::: {.spacer v-3f215769=""}
:::
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.content v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.content-container v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._twins_pricing style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

Pricing & Fees [​](pricing.html#pricing-amp-fees){.header-anchor} {#pricing-amp-fees tabindex="-1"}
=================================================================

The Twin.fun bonding curve determines deterministic pricing for every
trade.

Fees per Trade [​](pricing.html#fees-per-trade){.header-anchor} {#fees-per-trade tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------------

-   **Protocol fee** → sent to the treasury address.
-   **Subject fee** → paid to the twin owner.
-   Percentages are configured in the contract (`1e18 = 100%`).

Creation Rules [​](pricing.html#creation-rules){.header-anchor} {#creation-rules tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------------

-   At zero supply, buyers must purchase at least `minSharesToCreate`
    keys.
-   If an ID is pre-mapped to an owner, only that address can execute
    the first buy.

Pricing Intuition [​](pricing.html#pricing-intuition){.header-anchor} {#pricing-intuition tabindex="-1"}
---------------------------------------------------------------------

-   Earlier buyers pay less; each additional key marginally increases
    price.
-   Selling walks back down the curve, returning ETH at the lower supply
    point.

Mathematics (Advanced) [​](pricing.html#mathematics-advanced){.header-anchor} {#mathematics-advanced tabindex="-1"}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

Let `supply` be the current supply and `amount` the delta for the trade.

::: {.language- .vp-adaptive-theme}
[]{.lang}

``` {.shiki .shiki-themes .github-light .github-dark .vp-code tabindex="0"}
sum1 = (supply-1)*supply*(2*(supply-1)+1)/6         // if supply > 0 else 0
sum2 = (supply+amount-1)*(supply+amount)*(2*(...)+1)/6
price = (sum2 - sum1) * 1 ether / 1,600,000
```
:::

-   **Buy:** `getPrice(supply, amount)`
-   **Sell:** `getPrice(supply - amount, amount)`
-   **Fees:**
    -   `protocol = price * protocolFeePercent / 1e18`
    -   `subject = price * subjectFeePercent / 1e18`

</div>
:::
:::

::: {.content v-39a288b8=""}

::: {.main role="main" v-39a288b8=""}
::: {.vp-doc ._help_glossary style="position:relative;" v-39a288b8=""}
<div>

OpenGradient Glossary [​](glossary.html#opengradient-glossary){.header-anchor} {#opengradient-glossary tabindex="-1"}
==============================================================================

-   **DA** - **D**ata **A**vailability layer.
-   **Enclave nodes** - fully isolated virtual machines that are highly
    protected by a single security perimeter. They have no persistent
    storage, no external networking, no interactive access.
-   **Facilitator** - optional services on Base Sepolia that handle
    payment verification and payment settlement complexity for x402 LLM
    inference. Facilitators provide payment verification, payment
    settlement management, payment method abstraction, rate limiting,
    and receipt generation. Proof settlement and verification happen
    separately on the OpenGradient network.
-   **HACA** - **H**ybrid **A**I **C**ompute **A**rchitecture
-   **IModelExecutor** - a Solidity interface that contracts must
    implement to use Model Scheduler. It requires two functions: `run()`
    for executing model inference logic and `getInferenceResult()` for
    returning the latest model output.
-   **Inference** - a process of running a specific AI/ML model with a
    given input
-   **Inference Network** - OpenGradient\'s open network of inference
    nodes.
-   **Inference Node** - Nodes with specialized hardware, such as GPUs,
    responsible for model execution on OpenGradient.
-   **LLM** - **L**arge **L**anguage **M**odel - a type of AI model
    designed to understand and generate human-like text based on natural
    language prompts.
-   **ML** - **M**achine **L**earning - a subset of artificial
    intelligence that enables systems to learn and improve from
    experience without being explicitly programmed.
-   **Model Output** - the result returned from a model inference
    execution, containing the model\'s predictions or generated outputs.
-   **Model Scheduler** (AlphaSense) - enables scheduling and automating
    recurring model inference tasks on the OpenGradient blockchain.
    Contracts implementing `IModelExecutor` can be registered to execute
    automatically on a defined schedule.
-   **ONNX** - [**O**pen **N**eural **N**etwork
    **E**xchange](https://onnx.ai/) is an open standard format for ML
    models.
-   **PIPE** - **P**arallelized **I**nference **P**re-Execution
    **E**ngine - an on-chain inference execution method that allows
    models to be natively used from smart contracts.
-   **Settlement Modes** - three modes for x402 LLM inference settlement
    that determine what data is stored on-chain: `PRIVATE` (no data
    stored on-chain), `BATCH_HASHED` (aggregated Merkle tree with
    input/output hashes, default), and `INDIVIDUAL_FULL` (full model
    information, complete input/output data, and all inference
    metadata).
-   **TEE** - **T**rusted **E**xecution **E**nvironment - hardware or
    software security architecture that protects sensitive data/code
    from unauthorized access.
-   **Tensor** - a data structure that stores N-dimensional data.
-   **TPU** - Tensor Processing Unit, circuits specifically designed to
    accelerate ML workloads.
-   **VCS** - **V**alidation-**C**omputation **S**eparation
    architecture, where validator nodes are separated from inference
    nodes. This framework means that running inference on inference
    nodes does not have to be replicated on all validator nodes.
-   **x402** - an open, neutral standard for internet-native payments.
    It absolves the Internet\'s original sin by natively making payments
    possible between clients and servers, creating win-win economies
    that empower agentic payments at scale. x402 exists to build a more
    free and fair internet.
-   **ZKML** - **Z**ero-**K**nowledge **M**achine **L**earning - a
    combination of ZK and ML techniques, where running inference and
    training models are designed to generate zk-proofs that validate
    computation without revealing the input data.

</div>
:::
